title: Subtask effort/fast capping mutates a SHARED, cached provider — bleeds across parallel children and the orchestrator
problem: subAgent() calls applySubtaskEffort(prov,...) and applySubtaskFast(prov,...) which invoke prov.SetEffort()/SetFast() in place. But prov often comes from the router's provider cache (one *Provider per model id), so the SAME instance is handed to every subtask routed to that model. In a task_group fan-out, children sharing a model share one provider: a trivial child setting fast=true/effort=medium changes the tier+effort read by a concurrent sibling. When router returns nil with explicit trivial/easy difficulty, prov stays == a.provider() and the cap is applied to the ORCHESTRATOR's own provider.
fix: Only apply effort/fast discipline to a provider the subtask exclusively owns: skip apply* when prov == a.provider() or when prov came from the shared router cache, OR have the router hand out a fresh per-subtask provider clone.

title: Live page can panic indexing d.Live[cursor] during a pending stop confirmation
problem: The 1.2s livePoll refreshes d.Live every tick regardless of active page. liveState.confirmStop is set when cur()!=nil, but a subsequent poll can shrink d.Live before the next render. view() sets s.list.count=len(d.Live) but never calls clamp() before the confirmStop branch, which does in := d.Live[s.list.cursor] — an out-of-range panic if the highlighted session was removed by the daemon while the confirm prompt is up.
fix: Call s.list.clamp() right after s.list.count = len(d.Live) in view() (and on livePollMsg in app.go), and guard the confirmStop branch with if cursor >= 0 && cursor < len(d.Live) before indexing; cancel confirmStop and show a notice if the target vanished.

title: Project drill-in promises feed items but never renders them, corrupting the cursor<->row mapping
problem: The project drill-in's update() sets p.inner.count = p.feedN + len(proj.Sessions) and resolves enter as c < p.feedN -> feed item else proj.Sessions[c-p.feedN]. But view() inside-mode renders ONLY proj.Sessions[i] and draws zero feed rows. So per-project feed items are invisible/unreachable; and when feedN>0 the visible session at row i is proj.Sessions[i] yet enter opens proj.Sessions[i-feedN] (wrong session). clickAt() adds a third disagreement using proj.Sessions[idx] with no offset.
fix: Render feed items first in view() inside-mode (loop feedFor(proj.Dir) then sessions), marking clickMap 0..feedN-1 as feed and feedN.. as sessions. Make clickAt() resolve the same way. Recompute p.feedN in view() so count, render, click, enter share one index space.

title: Daemon bg-task count never wired — GUI always shows 0 agents
problem: Host.SetBgCount is the injector that fills DaemonStats.BgTasks, but daemon.go never calls it (grep: zero callers in production). So host.bgCount stays nil and Stats() leaves BgTasks at 0 forever. That zero is plumbed end-to-end to the GUI, which surfaces it in three prominent places — Home strip (agents count, Home.svelte:132), the Rail badge (Rail.svelte:51 returns daemon.stats.bg_tasks for the agents route), and the Observe KPI card with a live highlight (Observe.svelte:112). Result: even while background subtasks are actively running, the desktop GUI permanently reports 0 agents and never lights the live indicator. The capability fully exists (agent.LoadBgTasks(agent.TasksDir()) / BgRegistry.List() already power the Agents() bridge correctly), it's just not connected to the stats reporter.
fix: In runDaemon (daemon.go), after constructing host, add host.SetBgCount(func() int { n := 0; for _, t := range agent.LoadBgTasks(agent.TasksDir()) { if t.Status == running { n++ } }; return n }). agent.TasksDir() is already instance-aware. Makes Home/Rail/Observe show the real running-agent count.

title: GUI cannot install skills (InstallFromPath/InstallFromGitHub unreachable)
problem: skill.InstallFromPath / InstallFromGitHub (local-path and GitHub installs with security scanning) are wired in the CLI and the TUI app, but the GUI bridge exposes ONLY Skills/SkillBody/AcceptSkill/RejectSkill. There is no bound method to install a skill, so the Skills gallery's only guidance is the empty-state text Add one with eigen skill add. The desktop app forces users back to the terminal for a capability the codebase already implements.
fix: Add Bridge methods InstallSkillFromPath(src) and InstallSkillFromGitHub(ref) that call skill.InstallFromPath/InstallFromGitHub with InstallOptions{Dir, Scanner, Force/Overwrite}, surfacing RiskyError reasons. Bind in bridge.ts and add an Add skill affordance to Skills.svelte.

title: retrieve always builds an embedder pointing at localhost:8181, hammering it every Sync/Search when no embed server runs
problem: retrieveRunner does emb, _ := llm.NewEmbedder() and discards the configured bool. NewEmbedder DEFAULTS EIGEN_EMBED_BASE_URL to http://127.0.0.1:8181 and returns a NON-nil embedder unless explicitly empty. So with no local BGE service, the index identity becomes model=bge-small-en-v1.5 (not bm25), every Sync attempts an HTTP embed per changed file (failing) AND every Search issues a query-embed HTTP call to the dead endpoint.
fix: Honor the bool: emb, ok := llm.NewEmbedder(); if !ok { emb = nil }. Better, only treat the embedder as present after a one-time reachability probe (or when EIGEN_EMBED_BASE_URL is explicitly set). Cache the query-embed failure.

title: Custom reasoning models expose effort in the catalog but the config is never sent and effort can't be changed
problem: CustomModel carries Reasoning/Effort/EffortLevels and customModels() surfaces them into the catalog, so the picker shows the model as a reasoning model with effort levels. But none of the three custom adapters implement llm.EffortSetter, and customOpenAIResponses.Complete builds a responsesRequest with NO Reasoning field. A user defining a custom GPT-5-class endpoint with effort=high never gets a reasoning.effort field on the wire — purely cosmetic, and /effort silently no-ops. The custom-responses adapter also never sets Store/Include so reasoning continuity is impossible.
fix: In newCustomOpenAIResponses, when cm.Reasoning attach reasoningConfig{Effort, Summary} to the payload, store effort on the adapter, and implement SetEffort/Effort (validating against cm.EffortLevels). Mirror mantle's pattern.

title: Native Anthropic and Bedrock Converse never stream — the default Claude path shows no incremental output
problem: Neither Converse nor native Anthropic implements llm.Streamer (only Complete). The agent streams only when prov.(llm.Streamer) succeeds, otherwise falls back to a blocking Complete that emits NOTHING until the whole turn finishes. Converse's default model is us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8 — the daily-driver path — and a reasoning-heavy opus turn can take tens of seconds with a frozen spinner. mantle/codex/grok/glm/llama all stream; the most-used Claude backends don't.
fix: Implement Stream(ctx, req, sink) on both. Bedrock Converse has a converse-stream endpoint (contentBlockDelta events); native Anthropic Messages supports stream:true SSE. Reuse httpStream and add an SSE assembler mirroring parseResponsesSSE forwarding ChunkText/ChunkReasoning deltas.

title: Model-assisted compaction injects a synthetic user summary right before another user round, producing two consecutive user turns that Bedrock/Anthropic reject
problem: In CompactWith, keepFrom is always a user-round start so recent[0] is RoleUser. The function prepends a synthetic Message{Role: RoleUser} (the summary). Result: [RoleUser(summary), RoleUser(recent[0]), ...] — two adjacent user turns. converseMessages/anthropicMessages only group consecutive RoleTool into one user block. Bedrock Converse returns a ValidationException for two consecutive user turns. The default model (us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8) runs on converse, so the first model-assisted compaction of a long session can fail the next Complete().
fix: Make the summary a synthetic ASSISTANT turn, or merge it into recent[0]'s text the way deterministic Compact() does (recent[0].Text = injected + \n\n + recent[0].Text). Alternatively add a coalesce pass merging consecutive same-role messages.

title: GUI can view but not add/remove bans — banthis layer is half-wired
problem: memory.Store exposes AddBan/RemoveBan/ListBans (the native banthis hard-prohibition layer) and the TUI wires them via /ban and /unban. The GUI Memory view renders the bans block read-only with an enforced badge but offers no way to add or remove a ban — there are no Bridge.AddBan/RemoveBan methods. A capability reachable from the TUI is not reachable from the desktop GUI.
fix: Add Bridge.AddBan(scope, title, rule) and Bridge.RemoveBan(scope, title) delegating to the opened store, expose in bridge.ts, and add an inline add/remove affordance to the bans section in Memory.svelte.

title: GUI memory save never triggers consolidation/summary (bypasses Append)
problem: Bridge.AppendMemory calls s.AddAdHocNote(note, time.Now()) directly instead of s.Append(note). AddAdHocNote only writes the note file; it is Store.Append() that also calls enqueueMaintenance() to enqueue JobConsolidate + JobSummary. So notes saved from the GUI's Memory view are written but NEVER enqueue the jobs that fold them into MEMORY.md / regenerate memory_summary.md. The GUI shows note saved success.
fix: Change AppendMemory to call s.Append(note) (which redacts, writes the ad-hoc note, and enqueues consolidate+summary) rather than s.AddAdHocNote.

title: Dreaming agent should consolidate wider chunks of work, not just eigen + last session
problem: internal/dream Distill/Consolidate currently scoped to eigen + last session; widen to span more sessions/projects/transcripts.
fix: internal/dream Distill/Consolidate currently scoped to eigen + last session; widen to span more sessions/projects/transcripts.

title: Large MCP responses (>8 MiB line) silently kill the whole client for the session
problem: readLoop scans newline-delimited JSON-RPC with bufio.Scanner capped at an 8 MiB token. A single oversized response line (chrome_snapshot, full-page extracts, a base64 screenshot) makes sc.Scan() return false with bufio.ErrTooLong. The loop exits, the deferred block closes EVERY pending channel (so all in-flight AND future tool calls return connection closed), and sc.Err() is never inspected. The lazyClient still thinks it has a live *Client. One big tool result permanently bricks that MCP server for the session.
fix: After the loop, check sc.Err(): if bufio.ErrTooLong, surface a clear error. Raise the buffer cap (32-64 MiB) or switch to length-prefixed/streaming json.Decoder reads. Also mark lazyClient.client dead when the read loop ends so get() reconnects.

title: Plugin HTTP/SSE (URL) MCP servers are discovered and written but the loader can never run them
problem: The plugin layer fully supports remote MCP servers: discoverMCP/discoverApps parse URL and Type (http/sse), and addMCPServer writes url and type keys into mcp.json. But mcp.serverConfig has only Name/Command/Env/Description/Tools/ExcludeTools/Disabled — no URL or Type. LoadTools rejects any entry with len(Command)==0, and Connect only ever exec.Command's a subprocess. A plugin shipping an HTTP/SSE MCP server installs successfully but at session start the user gets a stderr error and the tools never appear.
fix: Either (a) implement an HTTP/SSE MCP transport (add URL/Type to serverConfig and a streamable-HTTP client branch in Connect/LoadTools), or (b) make the plugin installer refuse/skip URL-only MCP servers with a clear Warning instead of writing a config entry the loader silently drops.

title: Plugin hooks (auto-run shell) and MCP servers (subprocess commands) bypass the security scanner
problem: InstallPlugin runs opts.Scanner.Scan only on skills, slash commands, and agents. Hooks and MCP servers are wired with zero scanning. Hooks turn a plugin's hooks.json command into a shell invocation that runs AUTOMATICALLY on tool_start/tool_result/session_* events — no model, no approval. Yet ScanStatus is computed only from skill/command/agent verdicts, so a plugin shipping a malicious PreToolUse hook still records ScanStatus=clean and the UI shows a reassuring scan clean badge.
fix: Pass each hook command and each MCP server command through opts.Scanner before wiring, accumulating ScanFindings and honoring the RISKY-aborts-unless-Force rule. At minimum, when hooks/non-allowlisted MCP servers are present and unscanned, force ScanStatus to forced/skipped rather than clean and add a Warning.

title: Default chat model differs between the daemon path (Opus) and the documented/in-process path (gpt-5.5)
problem: With no config and no flags, the DEFAULT interactive chat goes through the daemon: main.go calls dc.NewSession(cwd, *model, *perm, history) with *model=empty and never passes *provider. The daemon build closure does prov := firstNonEmpty(cfg.Provider, converse) then mdl := firstNonEmpty(model, cfg.Model)=empty -> llm.DefaultModel(converse) = us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8. But the --model flag help says default: openai.gpt-5.5 and the in-process fallback defaults to mantle -> gpt-5.5. Same bare eigen picks Opus via daemon but gpt-5.5 in-process.
fix: Pick ONE default provider/model and use it on both paths. Either make the daemon build default to mantle/gpt-5.5 to match flag help, or change the flag default + docs to converse/opus. Simplest: pass the resolved *provider/*model when creating the daemon session.

title: Workflow per-step model: override runs an isolated subtask, breaking the carried-session contract
problem: The workflow package documents that steps run on ONE carried session so step N sees prior steps work, and model: is a first-class step directive. But runWorkflowHeadless does if model != empty { return a.SubtaskWith(ctx, prompt, SubtaskOpts{Model: model}) }. SubtaskWith spawns a FRESH sub-agent with no carried history — so any step with a model: override sees none of prior output. Worse, SubtaskWith promotes long-running subtasks to background and returns a placeholder string that becomes the step's output fed to the judge. The in-TUI runner ignores model: entirely.
fix: Make per-step model override carry context: send the prompt on the same carried session after a live model switch (swap the session provider, then restore), or drop model: support and document it unsupported. Never let SubtaskWith's background placeholder become a step output. Update the TUI note that model: is ignored in-chat.

title: Multiple un-synchronized session.Store instances write the same sessions.json concurrently
problem: The GUI opens fresh session.Store instances on demand — guiProjectDirs() and every ExportSession() each do session.Open()+Discover(), plus the app's long-lived store. Each Store has its OWN sync.Mutex but they all persist to the same sessions.json via save(). The per-instance mutex provides zero cross-instance synchronization, so saves can interleave and corrupt or lose the index.
fix: Share ONE session.Store across the GUI process (open once at startup, pass the handle into the Bridge). Have ExportSession and guiProjectDirs use that shared store. If impractical, serialize writes with a file lock (flock) around save().

title: Store.save() is a non-atomic os.WriteFile — a crash or concurrent writer truncates the whole session index
problem: save() marshals the entire index then calls os.WriteFile(indexPath, ...), which truncates-then-writes in place. If the process is killed mid-write (or two Store instances write at once) the file is left truncated, losing the ENTIRE session catalog (titles, cwd, ingested flags, fingerprints) for every discovered session. This is the single source of truth for the sessions list across TUI app and GUI.
fix: Write to a temp file in the same dir then os.Rename over indexPath (atomic on POSIX): write sessions.json.tmp, fsync, rename. Crash-safe and gives last-writer-wins instead of corruption.

title: Proactive ideas should be deduped and wider than one last session
problem: feed scanSuggest / dedup is narrow (eigen + last session). Ideas should dedupe across runs and draw from a wider span of work/projects.
fix: feed scanSuggest / dedup is narrow (eigen + last session). Ideas should dedupe across runs and draw from a wider span of work/projects.

title: apply_patch renames are completely broken (reads the non-existent destination)
problem: For a rename (*** Move to:/*** Rename to:, or a unified diff whose oldPath != newPath without /dev/null), the file falls into the default branch where target = f.newPath and the code does os.ReadFile(resolved) on newPath — which does not exist yet. A pure-rename agent patch returns no such file or directory and writes nothing. The old content at oldPath is never read, so every rename via apply_patch fails. renaming() helper is defined but never used.
fix: In the default branch, read from f.oldPath (the source): data, err := os.ReadFile(resolveOldPath). Apply hunks, write to newPath, the existing os.Remove(oldResolved) finishes the move. Add a pure-rename test.

title: atomicWrite destroys file permissions on every edit/write/patch
file: internal/tool/fsutil.go:atomicWrite (used by edit.go, multiedit.go, write.go, patch.go)
problem: atomicWrite writes to os.CreateTemp (mode 0o600) then os.Rename over the target. It never copies the original file's mode, so any existing file the agent edits is silently reset to 0o600. Editing a 0o755 shell script leaves it 0o600 — the exec bit and group/other read are stripped. This breaks executable scripts, hooks, and any non-default-mode file as a routine side effect.
fix: Before the Rename, stat the destination if it exists and chmod the temp file to the original's mode (os.Stat(path) -> tmp Chmod(fi.Mode().Perm())). For brand-new files keep 0o644.

title: Codex importer silently drops tool output when output is a JSON list (image/structured blocks)
problem: The Codex payload struct declares Output string, but in real rollout files the function_call_output.output field is frequently a JSON array of content blocks, not a string. Surveying ~50-80 local Codex sessions, 98 of ~2826 function_call_output lines have a list-shaped output. When output is a list, json.Unmarshal into the string field leaves it empty, so the tool result message imports with empty Text — the tool's actual result content is lost on every Codex resume that contains image/structured tool output.
fix: Change Output to json.RawMessage and flatten it the way claudeResultText handles tool_result: if string use directly; if array of {type,text} blocks, concatenate text blocks. Reuse/parallel claudeResultText so all importers handle the string-or-block-list shape uniformly.

title: Rotating .bak backups are written on every Save but never read back for recovery
problem: Save() maintains 5 generations of .bak backups (rotateBackups + backupPath), and the daemon even deletes them on session removal. But backupPath has no callers outside rotateBackups, and Load() opens only the primary path — no code ever falls back to a backup when the live transcript is missing/corrupt. The whole backup-generation machinery is write-only: it costs an extra read+rewrite per autosave yet provides zero recovery value.
fix: Wire recovery into Load (or add a RecoverFromBackup helper): when the primary file is missing, zero-length, or yields zero messages while a non-empty .bak exists, fall back to the newest readable backup generation in order.

title: Sessions should include other agents work (Claude Code, Codex) + resume after translating
problem: transcript readers for claude/codex/opencode/etc already exist in internal/transcript but eigen sessions do not ingest them; eigen should be able to translate an external agent transcript into an eigen session and resume it.
fix: transcript readers for claude/codex/opencode/etc already exist in internal/transcript but eigen sessions do not ingest them; eigen should be able to translate an external agent transcript into an eigen session and resume it.

title: Background task can run (and bill) up to 2x bgMaxRuntime — the cap is per-attempt, not per-task
problem: bgMaxRuntime (30m) is documented as the cap ensuring an abandoned task cannot run forever. But each attempt builds its own context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), bgMaxRuntime) inside runBackgroundAttempt (line 481). A context-overflow retry or a difficulty escalation runs a SECOND fresh 30m attempt, so a single SubtaskBackground can consume ~60m. The detached goroutine has no outer task-level deadline.
fix: Root all attempts of one task under a single context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), bgMaxRuntime) created once in SubtaskBackground (and promoteRunning's collector), deriving each attempt's context from it.

title: GUI AgentTranscript reads a disk path built from an unvalidated task id (path traversal)
problem: AgentTranscript(id) does filepath.Join(agent.TasksDir(), id+.transcript.jsonl) and os.ReadFile with no validation of id. Every other reader guards ids with agent.bgIDRe (^bg-[0-9]+-[0-9]+$). AgentTranscript is a Bridge method reachable from the frontend, so a crafted id like ../../../etc/passwd can read files outside the tasks dir. The agent package keeps bgIDRe unexported so the GUI has no shared validator.
fix: Export a validator from the agent package (func ValidTaskID(string) bool wrapping bgIDRe, or reuse BgRegistry.TranscriptPath) and have AgentTranscript reject ids that don't match before touching the filesystem.

title: Per-task attempt/escalation history is captured durably but never surfaced in the GUI
problem: BgRegistry.History(id) + readTaskHistory expose the full append-only state trail (attempt 1 failed -> escalating, attempt 2, context-overflow notes). The GUI's agents.go exposes Agents() and AgentTranscript() but has NO bridge method for History, so the GUI fan-out board can't show why a task retried/escalated. Data is on disk and read side implemented; not wired to the desktop front-end.
fix: Add a Bridge.AgentHistory(id) ([]BgTaskDTO, error) that validates the id and calls agent.readTaskHistory via an exported helper, mapping to BgTaskDTO; surface it in Agents.svelte expanded view as the attempt/escalation timeline.

title: Report/note truncation uses byte slicing that can corrupt multibyte UTF-8
problem: These cut strings with raw byte indexing — res[:maxGroupResultBytes], s[:max], s[:200], s[:160] — on text that routinely contains multibyte runes. Slicing mid-rune emits invalid UTF-8 into the fan-out report, the durable BgTask.LastNote/Error records, and the GUI/TUI panels. compactOversizedText already uses []rune and dispatch uses tool.TruncateUTF8.
fix: Replace the byte slices with rune-safe truncation — use tool.TruncateUTF8 or convert to []rune before slicing. Apply to formatGroupReport's per-child cap, oneScreen, sanitizeNote, truncateForNote.

title: List scroll window lags because update() and view() use different visible-row counts
problem: list.move()/key() advance list.top using visible = m.height - 6/7/8, but m.height is the FULL terminal height — the rendered area is l.inner.h further reduced by title/summary/detail. So update()'s visible is several rows larger than view()'s window. list.top advances later than view's smaller window can show, so on long lists/short terminals the selected row scrolls past the bottom while top stays put.
fix: Compute one canonical visible-rows value from computeLayout().inner.h minus the page's fixed chrome and use it in BOTH update() and view(). At minimum replace the m.height-N literals in update() with the h-N expression view() uses.

title: Skills, Models, Providers, Observe, and Crons rows are not mouse-clickable
problem: The shell runs with WithMouseCellMotion and every other list page implements clickAt for select/open. But Skills, Models, Providers, Observe, and Crons have selectable lists yet no clickAt and no dispatch in contentClick — clicking their rows silently does nothing, an inconsistent dead zone.
fix: Add clickAt methods to skillsState/modelsState/providersState/cronsState (select on first click, preview/open/toggle on second) and an observeState section click, then wire the missing cases into contentClick. Add clicks.mark(lineCount(out), i) in their view loops.

title: Wide-breakpoint inspector shows nothing for Live, Memory, Config, and Profile despite clear selections
problem: inspectorFor has cases for Sessions, Models, Providers, Crons, Plugins, Projects, Skills, Home, Machines, but not Live, Memory, Config, or Profile. On a >=130-col terminal the right inspector falls back to the generic select an item even when Live has a highlighted running session, Memory has a selected note, and Config has a selected field with a description.
fix: Add inspectorFor cases: Live -> selected d.Live[cursor]; Memory -> selected bullet; Config -> fields[cursor] (key, value, type/options, Desc); Profile -> usage totals. Reuse existing detail helpers.

title: j/k do not scroll the Observe and Profile pages, leaving long content reachable only via PgDn
problem: observeState.update calls s.list.key(j/k) which moves a phantom cursor that view() never reads (observe renders all sections top-to-bottom and relies on contentScroll), so j/k are consumed but scroll nothing. The shell's handleContentScrollKey only handles pgdown/pgup/ctrl+d/u/f/b/home/end — not j/k. Profile likewise renders long content but consumes nothing for j/k.
fix: Extend handleContentScrollKey to also handle j/k as +/-1 line scroll for pages that don't own a row cursor (or map j/k to scrollContent in observeState.update). Apply to profile too.

title: Cumulative token / cache-hit stats reset to 0 on every daemon restart
problem: cumIn/cumOut/cumCacheRead/cumCacheWrite are accumulated purely in-memory from EventDone in dispatch, and Restore() rebuilds every persisted session as a cold row with these counters at 0. The GUI treats CacheReadTokens/InputTokens as the headline token-efficiency metric. After any daemon restart the cache-hit ratio collapses to 0% and slowly climbs again, reading as a regression.
fix: Persist the per-session cumulative token counters in the sidecar persistMeta (host.go saveSessionMeta) and reload them in Restore() so the stats survive restart, OR relabel the GUI metric as since daemon start explicitly.

title: Eigen needs versions: know what version of daemon and gui/tui is running
problem: llm.Version=0.1.0 is a single const; daemon protocol has a Version field. Wire real versioning so daemon + gui/tui report their running version (handshake + display).
fix: llm.Version=0.1.0 is a single const; daemon protocol has a Version field. Wire real versioning so daemon + gui/tui report their running version (handshake + display).

title: Pending approval is double-emitted to a view that attaches mid-wait
problem: When a tool call is blocked on approval, installApprover broadcasts an EventApproval via dispatch (session.go:526), which lands in the replay buffer. On a fresh attach, server.go first replays the buffer (sending the approval event with Replay=true) AND then separately re-sends every pendingList() entry as a non-replay approval event. So a mid-wait attach receives the same approval twice. chat.Remote maps both approval events straight to agent.EventApproval with no dedup by approval id, so the TUI can render/prompt the same approval twice.
fix: In the attach handler, skip replay events whose Kind is approval (re-sent canonically from pendingList), or skip the pendingList re-send for approvals already present in the replay buffer.

title: interrupt op reports success even when nothing was interrupted
problem: The interrupt op calls sess.interrupt() (a no-op when s.cancel is nil) and unconditionally replies Type:ok. A view gets a success response whether or not a turn was actually cancelled, so it cannot distinguish interrupted a running turn from there was nothing to interrupt. interrupt is the one mutating op that does NOT go through withLiveSession, so a cold/unloaded session silently swallows the interrupt.
fix: Have Session.interrupt() return a bool (true only when s.cancel != nil and was invoked) and surface it on the Response (e.g. an Interrupted field).

title: BM25 rank() scores every chunk against the full query on each Search (O(chunks x queryTerms)) with no posting-list shortcut
problem: rank() iterates all N chunks and, for each, loops over every query term doing a map lookup — even though most chunks contain none of the query terms. For a 4000-file repo chunked at ~40 lines this is tens of thousands of chunks scored on every retrieve call. The vector path similarly does a full cosine scan.
fix: Build an inverted index (term -> []chunkIdx) in buildBM25 and, in rank(), accumulate scores only over the union of posting lists for the query terms. Turns the cost from O(NxQ) into O(matching postings).

title: GUI Skills gallery never live-refreshes new dream proposals or out-of-band installs
problem: Skills.svelte loads via Bridge.Skills() only inside $effect on mount; nothing emits a skills/proposals-changed event. The daemon dream pipeline calls skill.Propose() at arbitrary times, and eigen skill add can happen in another window, but the GUI shows the stale snapshot until the user navigates away and back. The feed has eigen:feed push; skills have no analogous event.
fix: Add an eigen:skills event: emit after AcceptSkill/RejectSkill (and after a dream Propose if the daemon can signal), and have Skills.svelte subscribe and call load(). At minimum refresh on window focus / route re-entry.

title: Propose() doc/behavior contradiction and silent overwrite of an unreviewed proposal
problem: The Propose comment says Overwrites an existing proposal of the same name but it only guards against an ACTIVE skill of that name; it unconditionally os.WriteFile's over any existing PROPOSED skill. The daemon dream loop can re-run and silently replace a proposal the user was about to review, with no diff/notice or timestamp.
fix: Either preserve the first proposal (refuse overwrite) or stamp proposals with a modtime and surface updated state. Reconcile the comment, the map page, and behavior; consider not re-proposing an identical body.

title: RescanFeed spawns unbounded concurrent scans with no in-flight guard (cache temp-file race)
problem: RescanFeed does go b.scanFeed() with no guard. The command-palette Refresh feed verb can be triggered repeatedly; each starts a full 2-minute scan (git probes, gh calls, a 90s LLM suggester) and N run concurrently. They all race to rename the same feed.json + feed-suggest.json temp files (fixed .tmp path).
fix: Add an atomic CAS scanning guard in scanFeed so RescanFeed is a no-op (or coalesces) while a scan is in flight; OR give save()/saveSuggestCache a unique temp name. The feedLoop ticker should share the guard.

title: Suggest cache is keyed only by time, not by the project-dir set — stale suggestions for removed projects persist up to 90 min
problem: loadSuggestCache returns the cached items as long as time.Since(Scanned) < suggestTTL (90 min) regardless of whether the dirs passed to scanSuggest changed. If the user removes a project, scanSuggest returns cached.Items for the OLD dirs while fresh. parseSuggestions validates dirs only on a fresh model call, never on cache reuse.
fix: Include a hash of the sorted dir set in suggestCache and treat the cache as stale when the signature changes, or drop cached items whose Dir is not in the current dirs before returning.

title: dirtyFiles miscounts the working tree when status output contains renames
problem: dirtyFiles counts lines of git status --porcelain, but a rename is reported on a SINGLE porcelain line as R old -> new (two paths, one line), and without -z a path containing a newline splits a single entry across lines. The headline feed item (N uncommitted files) can under/over-report. Since Item.Key() derives from the Title which embeds this count, a miscount also affects dismissal stability.
fix: Use git status --porcelain -z and count NUL-separated records (handling the rename record's embedded second path).

title: Custom OpenAI-Responses and custom Anthropic providers don't stream while custom OpenAI-chat does
problem: Only customOpenAIChat implements Stream. customOpenAIResponses and customAnthropic implement Complete only, so the agent's Streamer check fails and they fall back to a blocking Complete with no deltas — the same dead-air UX as the Converse/Anthropic gap but for user-defined providers. The wire assembler already exists (parseResponsesSSE).
fix: Add Stream methods: customOpenAIResponses POSTs stream:true to /responses via httpStream and assembles with parseResponsesSSE; customAnthropic reuses a native Anthropic SSE assembler. Share the assembler.

title: Custom OpenAI-Responses provider doesn't retry the empty-completed-response quirk that mantle handles
problem: customOpenAIResponses.Complete does a single httpJSON + parseReply with no retry, unlike Mantle.Complete which loops up to maxEmptyRetries when a completed response has empty Text and no ToolCalls (the Bedrock Responses quirk). Custom Responses endpoints target the same surface and are subject to the same quirk, but an empty completion is returned verbatim as a do-nothing turn.
fix: Reuse the same empty-retry loop as Mantle.Complete (post/parseReply bounded by maxEmptyRetries) in customOpenAIResponses.Complete.

title: Native Anthropic enables interleaved-thinking beta but drops thinking blocks across turns
problem: headers() always sends anthropic-beta interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14 and Complete parses incoming thinking blocks into out.Reasoning. But anthropicMessages never re-emits a thinking block on the assistant turn — anthropicContent has no signature field and the rebuilt assistant message contains only text + tool_use. With interleaved thinking + tool use, Anthropic expects the prior assistant turn's signed thinking block back; dropping it loses chain of thought and with strict signature validation can be rejected.
fix: Add a thinking-block type to anthropicContent (type, thinking text, signature) plus a signature carrier on Response/Message, capture the signature on parse, and re-emit the prior thinking block (preceding tool_use) when thinking is enabled. If signatures aren't captured, document non-continuity or drop the beta.

title: Model discovery for the converse/Bedrock provider resolves AWS creds with a different profile than the converse backend actually uses
problem: listBedrockModels uses loadAWSCreds(firstNonEmpty(AWS_PROFILE, default)), but the real converse backend and converseAvailable() both use firstNonEmpty(EIGEN_CONVERSE_PROFILE, AWS_PROFILE, aviary). A user on the documented default (EIGEN_CONVERSE_PROFILE=aviary) has working chat but eigen models discovery probes the wrong account, and never honors AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK.
fix: In listBedrockModels resolve the profile the same way converse does — firstNonEmpty(EIGEN_CONVERSE_PROFILE, AWS_PROFILE, aviary) — and accept the Bedrock bearer token path.

title: catalog.Lookup prefix matching mis-resolves short/ambiguous model ids to the wrong catalog entry
problem: After exact-match, Lookup falls back to a bidirectional prefix test: HasPrefix(model, m.ID) || HasPrefix(m.ID, model), returning the FIRST match in iteration order. Because the second clause matches when the catalog id is longer, Lookup(gpt-5) returns gpt-5.5 (codex, effort xhigh, priority tier), Lookup(glm-4) returns glm-4.7. A truncated id silently inherits a different model's capabilities, flowing into ContextBudget, ModelEffortLevels, isCapable routing.
fix: Drop the HasPrefix(m.ID, model) direction. Keep only HasPrefix(model, m.ID) for region/version-qualified ids, and when multiple entries are prefixes of the query pick the LONGEST matching id.

title: router.go doc comments claim hard general tasks are NOT routed, but Route() (and its tests) force-route them to the best tier
problem: The Difficulty doc (hard keeps the user's current DEFAULT model), targetTier's comment, and Route's contract all describe a stay-put policy for DiffHard general. But Route() has no such guard: targetTier(DiffHard)=TierFrontier(4), no catalog model is tier-4, so the fallback loop picks the highest tier present (tier-3 opus/gpt). TestRouteHardGeneralRoutesToBestModel asserts this. So a hard general subtask under /route is silently moved off the active model.
fix: Update the comments to match implemented behavior (hard general routes to strongest capable tier), or add the guard: if Difficulty==DiffHard && Kind==TaskGeneral { return empty, false }. Align code, comment, and test.

title: GUI Dreaming page is read-only — no way to trigger a dream/consolidation
problem: The Dreaming view shows rollout history and consolidation diffs but is entirely passive. The memory.Pipeline (RunQueued / MaybeConsolidate / Run) is only invoked by the daemon nightly tick, TUI idle, and the eigen dream CLI. There is no GUI affordance to run a dream/consolidation on demand.
fix: Add a Bridge.DreamNow(scope) method that builds a memory.Pipeline with the same dream callbacks, drains the scope's queued jobs (or forces MaybeConsolidate + RegenSummary), and returns a short report. Wire a Dream now button into Dreaming.svelte; gate with a clear disabled state if no provider.

title: GUI memory/dreaming loads swallow open errors — failure looks like empty
problem: Memory() and Dreaming() discard the error from memory.Open()/OpenGlobal() (proj, _ := ...) and pass a possibly-nil store to scopeDTO/dreamScope, returning an empty DTO. memory.Open can fail (UserHomeDir error, MkdirAll permission). The frontend cannot distinguish a genuine load failure from a legitimately-empty store. This is exactly the load-failure-vs-empty distinction commit 7c6737f set out to fix.
fix: Capture and return the error from memory.Open/OpenGlobal (or set an error flag on the DTO) so the frontend can render an error state. At minimum return the error so the existing Svelte catch (toasts.error) fires.

title: RegenSummary leaves a stale memory_summary.md when memory is emptied
problem: RegenSummary early-returns (no-op) when MEMORY.md is empty/whitespace, and never deletes memory_summary.md. Store.Injected() prefers memory_summary.md whenever non-empty. So if a consolidation clears MEMORY.md, the previously-distilled summary keeps being injected into every prompt indefinitely — injected memory diverges from curated memory.
fix: In RegenSummary, when TrimSpace(mem) == empty, remove the summary file (os.Remove(p.Store.SummaryPath())) instead of returning silently. Optionally add a Store method to clear the summary.

title: splitNotes fragments structured MEMORY.md into mismatched cards
problem: The GUI splits MEMORY.md into note cards on blank-line (\n\n) boundaries. But after consolidation, dream.Consolidate produces structured markdown — ## Heading then a blank line then a bullet list. Splitting on blank lines severs each heading from its bullets, so the Notes list renders orphan ## Preferences cards separate from their content.
fix: Render the curated MEMORY.md as a single Markdown document (like Summary/Bans/Profile) rather than splitting into cards, or split on top-level headings (\n## ) keeping each heading with its body. Derive NoteCount from headings or bullets.

title: USER.md should be auto-created + maintained by eigen, not hand-written by the user
problem: memory.UserProfile()/WriteUserProfile exist but USER.md is user-authored. Eigen should auto-create + maintain it (learned prefs), while still giving the user an extra free-form window. Memory must include USER.md.
fix: memory.UserProfile()/WriteUserProfile exist but USER.md is user-authored. Eigen should auto-create + maintain it (learned prefs), while still giving the user an extra free-form window. Memory must include USER.md.

title: Failed lazy MCP connect is never cached, so every gated tool call re-spawns and waits 15s on a broken server
problem: get() only caches a successful *Client; on connect failure it returns the error and leaves c.client nil and c.closed false. If the server later fails to launch, every subsequent tool invocation re-enters get(), spawns a fresh process, and blocks up to connectTimeout=15s before failing again — repeated 15s stalls and a pile of half-started subprocesses.
fix: Cache a failure with a short cooldown (remember lastErr + a retry not before timestamp) so repeated calls fail fast for a few seconds, while still allowing eventual recovery.

title: GUI cannot enable/disable an installed plugin, though the registry, TUI, and Bubble Tea app all can
file: internal/gui/plugins.go:104-112 (RemovePlugin is the only plugin op)
problem: Registry.SetEnabled performs a reversible enable/disable of all of a plugin's wired components and is surfaced in the TUI and the app plugins page. The Wails GUI bridge exposes only Plugins(), SetMarketEnabled, RemoveMarketplace, RemovePlugin — there is NO Bridge.SetPluginEnabled. A GUI user's only option to stop a broken plugin is to fully uninstall it. InstalledPluginDTO doesn't even carry an enabled flag.
fix: Add Bridge.SetPluginEnabled(name, enabled) delegating to reg.SetEnabled, and add an Enabled bool (via pluginEnabled logic) to InstalledPluginDTO so the page can show and toggle state.

title: MCP tool error with empty text produces a blank, useless error message
problem: When a tools/call response sets isError=true, the code returns fmt.Errorf(%s, res.Text). Many servers signal an error via isError with detail only in non-text content (or empty text), or return isError:true with no content blocks. The returned error is the empty string, which bubbles to the agent as a tool failure with no message.
fix: When res.Text is empty on an isError result, substitute a meaningful fallback, e.g. fmt.Errorf(mcp tool %q reported an error (no message), name), and include any image/other-block presence and the server name.

title: Plugin agent role kind silently dropped for valid Eigen kinds (e.g. code, review)
problem: normalizeAgentKind only accepts general/search/vision/social and maps everything else to empty. The agent role machinery supports more kinds; a plugin that declares a reasonable kind in its agents/*.md frontmatter loses its routing hint with no warning, so the role lands generic. The allowed list is duplicated here rather than referencing the canonical kind list in internal/agent.
fix: Source the valid-kind set from internal/agent (a single exported list/validator) instead of a private hardcoded switch, or record a Warning on the install result when a declared kind is dropped.

title: Plugin install reports N MCP server(s) even when some servers could not be wired (URL-only / empty command)
problem: discoverApps drops a server when len(cmd)==0 && URL==empty (good), but discoverMCP keeps every parsed server including ones that produce a command-less, never-runnable mcp.json entry. The install result counts everything appended to res.Plugin.MCPServers with no distinction between wired-and-runnable vs wired-but-the-loader-will-reject-it.
fix: In InstallPlugin, detect MCP servers that have neither a usable command nor supported transport, exclude them from res.Plugin.MCPServers, and emit a Warning naming each skipped server. Mirror the discoverApps len(cmd)==0 guard for discoverMCP.

title: --provider flag is silently dropped on the default daemon-backed chat path
problem: The daemon NewSession protocol carries only Model/Perm/History — there is no Provider field. main.go's default daemon path passes *model and *perm but not *provider, and the daemon build resolves provider purely from cfg.Provider/converse. So eigen --provider grok is ignored on the default path: the session is built on converse. It only works when the provider is encoded into the model id.
fix: Either fold --provider into the model ref before NewSession (pass llm.Ref(*provider, *model)), or add a Provider field to the daemon Request/NewSession and honor it in the build closure. Folding into the ref is the smaller change.

title: Authored workflows and custom slash-commands are reachable in the TUI but not in the GUI
problem: internal/workflow (eigen run / TUI /workflow) and internal/command (Claude-compatible /<name> custom commands) are fully built capabilities surfaced in the TUI. The GUI bridge exposes Config/Routing/Skills/Plugins/Agents/Feed/Observe/Machines but has NO Workflow list/run or custom-command list/run methods, so a GUI-only user can't see or run any authored workflow or custom slash command.
fix: Add bridge methods mirroring the TUI: Workflows() + RunWorkflow(sessionID, name, vars) that drives wf.Run over the daemon session, and Commands() + RunCommand(sessionID, name, args) using command.Load(command.Dirs()...).Expand(). Wire a small GUI surface.

title: Headless prompt-file/stdin task is read but never re-validated as a directory, and a directory piped in becomes a chat task
problem: The directory-detection block runs only for the positional task. A task via --prompt-file or piped stdin that happens to be a single path is treated as a literal prompt. More concretely, the stdin branch only triggers when !appRequested && task==empty && !isatty(stdin), so when both a positional task AND piped stdin exist the stdin is dropped silently with no notice.
fix: Document/normalize precedence: when both a positional task and piped stdin are present, either append stdin to the task or emit a one-line stderr note that stdin was ignored. Keep directory-as-root limited to the positional arg.

title: telegram_token is settable via config.Set but rejected by /config <key> describe and absent from pickers
problem: config.Set and config.Get both handle telegram_token, but Fields() (and Keys()) omits it. So /config telegram_token <token> succeeds, but /config telegram_token (describe branch) reports unknown key telegram_token because FieldFor returns the zero Field. The set path and the describe path disagree about whether the key exists.
fix: Make the surfaces agree. Either add telegram_token to Fields() (with a free-text/secret marker so Get masks it), or remove it from Set/Get so it's truly file-only like skills_dirs. Adding a Field entry with a secret flag is cleaner.

title: Data race on *Meta fields between background titler and Discover
problem: TitleUntitled's worker goroutine reads m.Source/m.Origin via firstUserText() WITHOUT holding s.mu, and later writes m.Title under s.mu. Meanwhile Discover()'s peek pass writes m.Title/m.Cwd/m.Messages and upsert() rewrites m.Origin/m.OriginMod under s.mu. Both operate on the same *Meta pointers from the shared map, and Discover is called repeatedly. A genuine data race under -race.
fix: In TitleUntitled, snapshot the per-session fields the worker needs into local copies, and have the worker look the meta back up by id under s.mu when it writes the title. Run -race over a concurrent Discover+TitleUntitled scenario.

title: GUI ExportSession cannot export daemon-hosted sessions (app TUI can)
problem: Bridge.ExportSession always routes through store.Export(id, ...), which Load()s by store meta id. Daemon-persisted sessions have ids that are NOT in the session store (they live under daemon.PersistedTranscriptPath), so store.Get(id) returns nil and Export returns os.ErrNotExist. The TUI app explicitly forks: daemon-source rows export via transcript.Load(daemon.PersistedTranscriptPath(r.ID)). The GUI lost that branch.
fix: Mirror the app's branch in ExportSession: if the id is a daemon-persisted session, transcript.Load that path and transcript.Save to dest; otherwise fall back to store.Export.

title: WireEvent drops Provider/Model/cache-token fields, so remote sessions lose them on the live stream
problem: agent.Event carries Provider, Model, CacheReadTokens, CacheWriteTokens on EventDone, but WireEvent only has Kind/Step/Text/Tool*/Result/IsError/InTokens/OutTokens. So wireToEvent always produces EventDone with empty Provider/Model and zero cache tokens for daemon-hosted sessions. Any UI consumer wanting per-turn model attribution or the prompt-cache-hit signal gets nothing on remote sessions.
fix: Add Provider, Model, CacheReadTokens, CacheWriteTokens to WireEvent and populate them in wireEvent(); map them back in chat/remote.go wireToEvent. Purely additive omitempty fields.

title: firstUserText reads only the first 300 lines — Codex rollouts can bury the first user message and never get titled
problem: firstUserText caps the scan at 300 lines. Codex rollout-*.jsonl files prepend large blocks of non-message records before the first response_item message with role=user. If the first user message lands past line 300, firstUserText returns empty, the worker continues, and the session is left permanently untitled.
fix: Raise the line cap meaningfully for Codex (or make it source-specific), or scan until the first user message OR a byte budget is hit rather than a fixed small line count. Bump 300 to e.g. 5000.

title: Background-shell Started/Finished timestamps are dropped before the GUI/TUI
problem: tool.ShellInfo carries Started and Finished time.Time and Infos() populates them, but daemon.ShellInfo and chat.ShellInfo only declare ID/Command/Status/ExitCode/LastLine. session.go:619 copies the snapshot without Started/Finished, so the data is discarded at the wire boundary. The GUI shells panel can never show how long a shell has run or when it finished.
fix: Add Started/Finished (RFC3339 or unix) to daemon.ShellInfo and chat.ShellInfo, copy them through in session.go:619 and the local/remote chat adapters, expose in the GUI dto, and render elapsed/finished time.

title: grep/symbols/glob return ripgrep failures as successful results
problem: runRipgrep maps any rg ExitError to (output, exitCode, nil) so a genuine rg failure (exit 2: invalid regex) is not an error. grep/symbols only special-case code==1 (no matches); for code==2 they return rg's raw stderr as the tool's text result with err=nil. grep with pattern (unclosed returns rg: regex parse error as if it were search output.
fix: In grep/symbols/glob, treat code==2 (or any code>1) as an error: if code >= 2 { return error }. Or have runRipgrep return the exit error for codes other than 0/1.

title: todo tool parses priority but never uses it
problem: The todo schema advertises a per-item priority enum (high|medium|low) and the Run closure unmarshals Priority, but the field is never read: the rendered plan ignores it and the return string never surfaces it. The model is told it can prioritize tasks, supplies priorities, and they vanish.
fix: Either drop priority from the schema, or use it: sort/group by priority and/or include a priority marker in the rendered line.

title: Empty-message Save clobbers a good transcript with no recoverable path
problem: Save() unconditionally writes whatever slice it is given. If a caller passes an empty/short slice, rotateBackups first copies the current good transcript into .bak and then the atomic rename replaces the live file with an empty one. Combined with backups never being read back, an accidental empty save is permanent data loss of the conversation. No guard at the transcript layer.
fix: Guard Save against truncating an existing non-empty transcript with an empty/much-shorter message set: if len(msgs)==0 and the target already exists non-empty, refuse (return an error or no-op).

title: Foreign importers drop assistant Reasoning, so resumed Codex/Claude sessions lose chain-of-thought continuity
problem: llm.Message carries Reasoning/ReasoningID/ReasoningEncrypted so chain-of-thought is preserved across tool calls (client MUST carry the encrypted blob back or Codex 404s on the reasoning item id with store:false). The Codex parser ignores reasoning lines and the Claude parser ignores thinking blocks. Resuming a foreign session strips all prior reasoning; for Codex store:false this can break the very next turn.
fix: In parseCodex, capture reasoning response_item lines (summary text + encrypted_content) into the assistant message's Reasoning fields before flush. In parseClaude, fold thinking blocks into asst.Reasoning.

title: SaveMeta writes the meta sidecar non-atomically, risking a truncated/corrupt config on crash
problem: Save() for the transcript is atomic (temp+fsync+rename+dir sync) but SaveMeta uses a plain os.WriteFile, which truncates-then-writes in place. A crash mid-write (called every turn alongside autosave) leaves a half-written .meta.json; LoadMeta then returns ok=false and resume silently resets provider/model/perm/effort/search/goal/loop to launch defaults.
fix: Make SaveMeta atomic the same way Save is: write to a temp file in the same dir, Sync, Close, then os.Rename over metaPath.

title: claudeDirFromPath mis-decodes project folders containing hyphens or dotfiles, mis-grouping the Projects page
problem: claudeDirFromPath decodes Claude's -home-user-proj folder name to a cwd by naive strings.ReplaceAll(dir, -, /). Real folder names use -- to encode a literal - and encode dot-dirs as a leading-dash segment. -home-avifenesh--claude-action-graph decodes wrong, and -home-avifenesh-projects-agent-sh-ada-spark decodes to .../agent/sh/ada/spark. When a Claude line lacks an explicit cwd, this wrong path becomes the session's Cwd and groups it under a nonexistent project.
fix: Decode using Claude's actual scheme: split on single -, treat -- as an escaped literal hyphen. Or only use the folder-name fallback when it resolves to an existing directory (os.Stat), otherwise leave Cwd empty.

title: claudeResultText concatenates text blocks with no separator, mashing multi-block tool results together
problem: claudeResultText flattens a tool_result content list by appending each text block's bytes directly (b = append(b, blk.Text...)) with no separator. Two lines line one and line two become line oneline two. The same no-separator pattern affects assistant text folding (asst.Text += b.Text).
fix: Join text blocks with a newline separator when more than one block is present, e.g. collect into []string and strings.Join(parts, \n). Mirror wherever blocks are folded.

title: Empty-turn nudge is appended without persisting, unlike the reasoning-only path
problem: In drive(), the reasoning-only branch appends the carried-forward reasoning message AND calls s.persist(). The genuinely-empty branch appends a Continue: use a tool nudge and continues WITHOUT s.persist(). If the process crashes between the nudge append and the next persist, the in-memory history and the autosaved transcript diverge.
fix: Add s.persist() after appending the empty-turn nudge message, matching the reasoning-only and steer branches.

title: synthesizeReports runs outside the group's deadline
problem: TaskGroup derives a gctx bounded by groupTotalTimeout for the children, but the optional synthesis step calls a.synthesizeReports(ctx,...) with the PARENT ctx, not gctx. So after wg.Wait(), the synthesis sub-agent's model call is bounded only by the parent turn's context, allowing the task_group to exceed its own groupTotalTimeout budget. A slow synthesizer also isn't stall-watched.
fix: Pass a context bounded by the remaining group budget to synthesizeReports (a fresh context.WithTimeout off ctx, or reuse gctx if it still has time).

title: Crons view paginates from list.top with a different height than navigation, so long timer lists scroll inconsistently
problem: Crons view iterates for i := start (=c.list.top); i < len(rows) && i < start+visible with visible=h-8, while update scrolls with m.height-6. Crons also bypasses list.window() entirely and reads list.top raw, so when the cursor is moved to the end (G) the top may not follow and the active timer can render off-screen.
fix: Use from,to := c.list.window(visible) with the same visible value as update (after fixing the shared height mismatch), instead of the manual start:=c.list.top loop.

title: Live page n (new daemon session) ignores the configured default model
problem: The config page advertises its values as defaults for NEW sessions, but liveState n calls d.Daemon.New(cwd, empty), which lets the daemon pick its own default model rather than m.data.Config.Model. A user who set a default model gets a daemon session on a different model.
fix: Pass the configured default through: d.Daemon.New(cwd, m.data.Config.Model) (fall back to empty when unset). If the daemon default is authoritative, surface that in the page copy.

title: Title-bar overflow guard recomputes left to an identical value (dead branch)
problem: When left+right+1 exceeds title.w, the code reassigns left to the exact base value it already held before the purpose suffix may have been appended. It drops the purpose suffix but never trims the right side, so a too-wide right segment still overflows. The branch reads as a no-op and the real overflow case (wide right text) is unhandled.
fix: On overflow, drop the right-side action suffix first (rebuild rightText without the action) and only then strip the left purpose; or truncate(left+gap+right, title.w) explicitly. Remove the identical reassignment.

title: Dead Host.Count() and redundant llm import-guard
problem: Host.Count() has zero callers anywhere in the repo. And session.go:490 has var _ = llm.RoleUser // keep llm imported while the same file uses llm extensively, so the guard is pure dead weight and the comment is misleading.
fix: Delete Host.Count() and remove the var _ = llm.RoleUser line — the build cannot break since llm is genuinely used in session.go.

title: Stale persistMu comment claims a fixed tmp path that no longer exists
problem: persistMu is documented as serializing transcript writes that share a fixed tmp path, but transcript.Save uses os.CreateTemp with a unique .*.tmp name per write — there is no shared fixed tmp path. The mutex is still useful but the rationale comment is wrong.
fix: Update the persistMu comment to its real purpose: serialize concurrent transcript Save + .bak rotation for one session so a shutdown flush and an agent-loop Persist can't interleave writes/backups.

title: GitHub feed scan gives no signal when gh is unauthenticated vs absent — silently empty
problem: scanGitHub returns nil both when gh is missing AND when gh exists but is unauthenticated or the search errors (ghSearch swallows err -> nil). The user who has gh installed but not gh auth login gets an eternally empty GitHub feed with zero explanation.
fix: Distinguish the cases: when gh is present but the search fails with an auth error, emit a single low-priority github Item telling the user to run gh auth login. Keep the silent no-op only when gh is genuinely absent.

title: firstSentenceAround leaks the leading - bullet marker into memory feed titles
problem: When the intent match has no preceding .;: separator in the bullet, start=0 (LastIndexAny returns -1, +1 = 0), so the returned clause includes the bullet's leading -. The feed Title renders e.g. projname: - we still need to ship X with a stray dash.
fix: Strip a leading bullet marker (TrimPrefix - / TrimLeft -* \t) in firstSentenceAround before returning, or start the search after splitBullets strips it.

title: retrieve secret-file denylist misses common secret filenames
problem: denied() excludes .env / .env.* basenames and a fixed set of dir segments (.ssh/.aws/.gnupg), but does not exclude other common secret files: .envrc, *.pem, id_rsa / id_ed25519, *.key, credentials, .netrc, *.p12/.pfx. The index is what the model reads back and embeds to an embed service, so leaking these into snippets is a real exposure.
fix: Broaden denied()'s basename/extension checks to cover .envrc, *.pem, *.key, id_rsa*, id_ed25519*, credentials, .netrc, *.p12, *.pfx, and normalize rel to forward slashes before the segment split.

title: Codex has a doc comment for a SetSearch method that doesn't exist
problem: codex.go:149-150 carries a doc comment SetSearch is a no-op stub... but there is no SetSearch method below it — the comment floats above FastMode. It misleadingly implies Codex implements Searcher symmetry.
fix: Remove the orphaned comment (codex.go:149-150). Codex correctly is not a Searcher.

title: Dead methods: Converse.additionalFields, GLM.prepare, Grok.prepare are never called
problem: (*Converse).additionalFields() is unused — Complete calls package-level additionalConverseFields. Likewise (*GLM).prepare and (*Grok).prepare are dead: the Complete/Stream paths call package-level glmPrepare/grokPrepare with a snapshot. These thin wrappers add confusion about which is canonical.
fix: Delete the three unused methods (and the doc comment above additionalFields), keeping the package-level functions.

title: httpJSON silently truncates oversize responses, surfacing as a confusing JSON-decode error
problem: httpJSON reads the body with io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxResponseBytes) (16 MiB cap). If a provider returns a >16 MiB 2xx body, the read is silently cut mid-JSON and returned as valid (raw, status, nil); each provider then fails at json.Unmarshal with an opaque unexpected end of JSON input.
fix: Detect truncation: read with LimitReader of maxResponseBytes+1 and if the body hit the cap, return an explicit error like response exceeded NN MiB cap.

title: ShedToolImages and DedupeToolResults stubs are not recognized as already-stubbed by ShedToolResults/ShedOldToolResults, so notes get clobbered
problem: ShedOldToolResults and ShedToolResults only skip results whose Text is exactly toolResultStub or empty. A result previously rewritten to imagePrunedStub or duplicateResultStub is NOT treated as already-stubbed, so a later round-based shed overwrites it with the generic toolResultStub, discarding the more specific note and the dedupe pointer.
fix: Extend the already-stubbed guard in both functions to also skip when Text == imagePrunedStub || Text == duplicateResultStub (a small isStub helper).

title: gui/routing.go ProviderDTO struct is not gofmt-clean (misaligned tags)
problem: gofmt -l flags internal/gui/routing.go: the ProviderDTO field tags are misaligned. It compiles but fails a gofmt gate and is inconsistent with the rest of the package.
fix: Run gofmt -w internal/gui/routing.go (or align the struct fields/tags).

title: listBedrockModels comment claims it lists ListFoundationModels + ListInferenceProfiles, but only queries inference-profiles
problem: The function comment says it uses ListFoundationModels + ListInferenceProfiles and both are listed, but the body only calls the inference-profiles endpoint; ListFoundationModels is never invoked. Foundation models that lack an inference profile are never surfaced as new.
fix: Either add the ListFoundationModels call and merge its modelSummaries ids, or trim the comment to lists inference profiles.

title: Ban title is stored unredacted while the rule is redacted
problem: AddBan redacts the rule (Redact(rule)) but stores the title verbatim. bans.md is injected into every prompt (Section() frames bans as system-priority). A token in the title (e.g. a ban about a specific API key) would persist and inject unredacted, inconsistent with the redaction guarantee everywhere else.
fix: Apply Redact to the title as well: title = strings.TrimSpace(Redact(title)) before the empty check.

title: Bridge.MemoryBackups exposed but never called by the GUI
problem: MemoryBackups(scope) is implemented in the Go bridge and re-exported in bridge.ts, but no Svelte view ever calls it — the Memory view shows only the backup COUNT and the Dreaming view lists .bak snapshots via the Dreaming() DTO instead. The backup-path listing capability is dead-wired.
fix: Either wire MemoryBackups into a UI affordance (a view backups action that lets the user open/restore a snapshot) or remove the unused bridge method + bridge.ts entry.

title: orientation Coupled lacks the no-history guidance the other readers give
problem: Provenance, Related, Query, Threads all print a clear (no orientation history for this project) message when loadEpisodesForCWD returns no episodes. Coupled instead falls straight through to coupledPairs (returns nil) and prints only (no coupled files recorded). The agent cannot tell whether the project is un-indexed or the file genuinely has no co-edits.
fix: Add the same if len(eps) == 0 { print (no orientation history for this project yet); return nil } guard at the top of Coupled.

title: orientation status hardcodes lagging cursors to 0
problem: Status prints kv(lagging cursors, 0) — a literal 0, never computed. It counts cursor files via the _cursors glob just above but never derives how many are lagging, so the metric is always 0. It reads like a stubbed-out field.
fix: Either compute a real lagging-cursor count (compare cursor offsets against current source sizes) or drop the line entirely.

title: MCP server gist truncation can split a multibyte UTF-8 rune
problem: firstSentence truncates with the byte slice s[:117] when len(s) > 120. For a server whose instructions' first line is long and contains non-ASCII (accented text, CJK, emoji), this can cut through the middle of a multibyte rune, yielding a broken character at the end of the Level-0 group description.
fix: Truncate by runes, e.g. r := []rune(s); if len(r) > 120 { s = string(r[:117]) + ... }, matching internal/app/list.go.

title: Marketplace catalog filtering reuses the backing array via in-place slicing, risking aliasing
problem: Several spots use the out := slice[:0] then append idiom to filter in place. filterInstalledCatalog reuses p.catalog's backing array; when the filtered result is the same backing array referenced elsewhere, this writes over earlier elements while iterating. For the registry CRUD it's safe (freshly read), but filterInstalledCatalog mutating p.catalog in place is fragile.
fix: In filterInstalledCatalog, build a new slice (var out []PluginEntry) rather than aliasing p.catalog[:0], or guarantee no other reference exists.

title: .env parser keeps inline comments and trailing whitespace in unquoted values
problem: loadOne only strips FULL-LINE comments. A credential line like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... # rotate monthly keeps  # rotate monthly as part of the value, and trailing spaces survive. Because these are credentials, a stray inline comment silently corrupts the key and yields confusing auth failures.
fix: For UNQUOTED values, strip an inline  #... comment (split on the first unescaped  #) before trimming; leave quoted values verbatim.

title: Daemon/attach/remote TUI options pass raw cfg.MaxTokens while the local path resolves it
problem: The in-process local TUI passes MaxTokens: resolveUserMaxTokens(*maxTokens) (which honors EIGEN_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS and the flag), but daemon.go:attachTUI and remote_session.go:remoteAttachLoop pass raw cfg.MaxTokens. So an attach/remote view ignores EIGEN_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS and --max-tokens for the budget, unlike a fresh chat.
fix: Route all three through the same resolver: pass resolveUserMaxTokens(cfg.MaxTokens) in attachTUI and remoteAttachLoop.

title: Exported but unused public API: command.UserDir() and theme.NerdFont()
problem: command.UserDir() has zero callers repo-wide (only Dirs() enumerates command dirs), and theme.NerdFont() bool has zero callers (the active tier is read via NerdFontMode() and the internal nerdFont var). Both are public API with no consumer — dead surface.
fix: Remove command.UserDir() and theme.NerdFont(), or wire them where the duplication exists (have Dirs() call UserDir()). Lowest-risk: delete them.

title: GUI ObserveSummary DTO drops subagent fields the TUI app surfaces (status/promote/route/background notes)
problem: SubagentStatsDTO only carries TaskCalls/TaskErrors, GroupCalls/GroupErrors, MutatingCalls/MutatingErrors, BackgroundDone. The observe.SubagentSummary also has StatusChecks, Promotes, PromoteErrors, BackgroundNotes, RouteNotes — all rendered by the TUI app observe panel. The GUI observe view silently omits them, an app<->GUI parity gap in already-computed data.
fix: Add StatusChecks, Promotes, PromoteErrors, BackgroundNotes, RouteNotes to SubagentStatsDTO and populate from s.Subagents, then surface in the GUI observe view.

title: Local status bar shows Name() (with provider suffix); Remote shows ModelID() (raw) for the same model
problem: Local.SetModel sets l.modelID = p.Name() and Name() includes a human suffix like (zhipu glm) or (bedrock converse). Remote.SetModel sends p.ModelID() and Remote.ModelID() returns the raw model id. So the status-bar model string differs between a local chat and an attached daemon session running the identical model.
fix: Use p.ModelID() for the tracked modelID in Local.SetModel (and in NewLocal) so both backends display the same raw id; keep ProviderName() for the provider label separately.

title: Steer-while-turn-ending race on daemon sessions can double-send a message
problem: steerOrQueue runs only while the TUI believes a turn is running; it calls backend.Steer and, if false, QUEUES the message for the next turn. But the daemon's SteerInput STARTS A NEW TURN when no turn is running, returning steered=false. If the turn finishes between the TUI seeing running and the daemon receiving SteerInput, the daemon launches a fresh turn with the message AND the TUI queues the same message — sent twice.
fix: Make Remote.Steer never auto-start a turn (a steer-only daemon op returning false when idle), or have steerOrQueue treat steered=false from a remote backend as already started, do not queue. Cleanest: a dedicated inject-if-running op.

title: Dead code: findBlock and filePatch.renaming are unreferenced
problem: filePatch.renaming() and findBlock() are defined but never called. findBlock is the superseded single-match locator replaced by findHunk/findBlockMatches; renaming() looks like it was meant to gate rename handling that was never wired (and that rename path is broken).
fix: Delete findBlock entirely. Either delete renaming() or use it to drive a correct rename branch in applyPatch (which also fixes the rename bug).

title: memory add accepts an empty note silently
problem: For action=add with kind=note, the code calls store.Append(in.Note) with no check that Note is non-empty (only the ban path validates a title). A model call with action=add and an empty/whitespace note records an empty memory entry and returns noted for future sessions, polluting the store.
fix: Before Append, validate strings.TrimSpace(in.Note) != empty and return a clear error otherwise — mirroring how other tools guard required string inputs.

title: move fails across filesystems (os.Rename EXDEV) with no fallback
problem: move uses os.Rename, which returns EXDEV when source and destination live on different filesystems. An /add-dir'd root can be on another mount, so a legitimate move between two allowed roots fails with invalid cross-device link.
fix: On os.Rename returning EXDEV (errors.Is(err, syscall.EXDEV)), fall back to copy-then-remove (preserving mode), handling files and directories.

title: Discover re-peeks every session full-file on a peekVersion bump despite a 256KB head cap that doesn't bound turn counting
problem: scanPeek caps title/cwd extraction at peekMaxBytes (256KB) but the turn-count classifier runs over the WHOLE file on every peek. On a peekVersion bump, up to peekBudget=400 sessions are re-peeked per launch, each a full read + per-line JSON unmarshal of multi-MB transcripts just to recount turns.
fix: Persist the turn count alongside PeekVer keyed by origin mtime so an unchanged file is not re-counted on a peekVersion bump, or cap turn counting to a sampled estimate. At minimum decouple count-version from title/cwd-version.

title: peekHermes returns no cwd or title, leaving Hermes sessions ungrouped and untitled in the browser
problem: Every other peeker (claude/codex/pi/eigen) extracts both a cwd and a first-user-message title, but peekHermes only counts turns and returns Preview{Messages: total} with empty Cwd and Title. Hermes sessions therefore always land under the no-project bucket and show an empty title. parseHermes proves the user content is readily available.
fix: Implement a real peekHermes head-scan: parse the first user line for Content and run it through titleFrom for the Title; populate Cwd if available. Bring to parity with other peekers.

title: titleFrom rejects any user message starting with { or [, blanking legitimate titles
problem: titleFrom returns empty for any message that begins with <, {, or [, intending to filter injected XML/JSON blobs. But a real human ask can legitimately start with those (e.g. {count} should be reactive). Such sessions get a blank derived title and show up untitled.
fix: Tighten the heuristic: only reject when the message parses as valid JSON (json.Valid) or looks like a complete XML/instruction tag, rather than rejecting on the first byte alone.

title: GUI scroll very slow, especially scrolling up
problem: VirtualList recomputes the full offset array on every onscroll (unthrottled) and re-measuring not-yet-measured rows above the viewport shifts totalH/offsets, yanking the scroll anchor upward. Scrolling up is the worst case. Needs offset memoization / scroll throttle / anchor preservation.
fix: VirtualList recomputes the full offset array on every onscroll (unthrottled) and re-measuring not-yet-measured rows above the viewport shifts totalH/offsets, yanking the scroll anchor upward. Scrolling up is the worst case. Needs offset memoization / scroll throttle / anchor preservation.

title: Image/multimodal input is fully plumbed end-to-end but unreachable from the GUI
problem: The entire image pipeline exists: bridge.ts SendInput/SteerInput both take ImageDTO[], dto.go fromImageDTOs decodes base64 with a 16 MiB cap, and every LLM provider consumes msg.Images. Yet Chat.svelte's send() passes [] for images in BOTH the steer and fresh-turn paths, and the Composer has zero attach/paste/drop/clipboard support. The GUI literally cannot send a single image even though the daemon, bridge, and models all handle them.
fix: Add image intake to Composer (paste-from-clipboard + drag/drop + file picker), accumulate ImageDTO[] (mediaType + base64), and pass them through Chat.send() to Bridge.SendInput/SteerInput instead of the hardcoded []. Render thumbnails and clear after a successful turn.

title: Profile Usage tokens are volatile in-memory sums, not lifetime totals
problem: Profile presents itself as lifetime Usage and shows tokens in/out and cache hit from the live daemon.stats stream. But DaemonStats.InputTokens/OutputTokens/CacheReadTokens are summed over only sessions currently tracked in memory (host.go sums s.cumIn over h.sessions) — they reset to ~0 on daemon restart and shrink as sessions are pruned. They sit next to turns logged and errors which DO come from the durable observability log. So two KPIs use durable lifetime data and two use ephemeral session-scoped data under one Usage heading.
fix: Drive the token/cache KPIs from the durable log: sum summary.models[*].inTokens/outTokens/cacheReadTokens (already fetched) for true lifetime totals. Keep daemon.stats only for live counts. Or relabel the section Live session usage.

title: Subagent/delegation telemetry computed in Go but surfaced by no view
problem: ObserveSummaryDTO.Subagents is fully populated by the Go side (taskCalls/taskErrors, groupCalls/groupErrors, mutatingCalls/mutatingErrors, backgroundDone) and is in the TS type, but no Svelte view ever reads summary.subagents. Multi-agent fan-out is a headline capability (it has a whole Agents.svelte board), yet Observe's tabs are Routes/Tools/Models/Hooks/Errors with no delegation/subagent surface.
fix: Add a Subagents (or Delegation) tab to Observe.svelte's tabs and render summary.subagents: KPI cards for task vs group calls, a mutating-calls line, background-done count, and error counts per kind. Same summary is already loaded — no new fetch.

title: Approval gate hides the tool arguments — you Allow/Deny blind
problem: The awaiting-approval dock (lines 695-707) renders only ap.tool and Allow/Deny buttons. The backend DTO (ApprovalInfo = { id; tool; args }) carries the full tool arguments, but the GUI never shows them. A gated approval is precisely the moment the user needs to see WHAT is being run before approving. As shipped, the user grants or denies a tool call without any visibility into its effect — a correctness/safety gap.
fix: Render ap.args in the approve card, e.g. a <code> block / pre under .approve__tool showing the (truncated, expandable) args string. The data is already on sess.pending[].args. Match the shell card pattern.

title: Chat transcript shows nothing (no loading state) while State() is in flight
problem: On entering a session, attach() fires Bridge.State(id) asynchronously and the transcript starts empty. isEmpty is sess != null && sess.messages.length === 0, so while sess is still null the starter block is NOT shown, rows is [], and the main area renders a bare empty VirtualList with no spinner or skeleton. For a session with existing history on a slow daemon/ssh, the conversation pane is simply blank for the round-trip then pops in. Sessions/Live/Machines/Home all have skeletons; Chat's transcript has none.
fix: Track a loading flag in the lifecycle $effect (set true before attach(), cleared in the State().then/catch) and render a transcript skeleton or Loading conversation... state when loading && sess == null && rows.length === 0, distinct from the isEmpty starter state.

title: GUI should display daemon + gui version (wire llm.Version through the bridge)
problem: Rail footer shows 0.1.0 hardcoded-ish; wire the real daemon Version + gui build version through DaemonStats/bridge so the footer and an about surface are truthful.
fix: Rail footer shows 0.1.0 hardcoded-ish; wire the real daemon Version + gui build version through DaemonStats/bridge so the footer and an about surface are truthful.

title: DiffView and ToolCallCard diffs have no copy affordance
problem: DiffView is the showpiece detail surface and the renderer for ToolCallCard mutation diffs. CodeBlock has a copy button, but DiffView has zero clipboard affordance. A user reviewing a tool's file change cannot copy the patch, the added lines, or the path — the richest surface in the app is read-only with no extraction path.
fix: Add a small sans copy control in the diffstat eyebrow (mirroring CodeBlock's copy button + 1.4s confirm + onDestroy timer cleanup) that writes the raw patch (or added lines) to navigator.clipboard. Keep it pointer/keyboard accessible.

title: Popover never flips/clamps when the trigger sits low in the viewport
problem: place() positions the panel unconditionally at top: r.bottom + 6 and only measures left/right. The panel has max-height: 70vh and scrolls internally, but it is anchored to the trigger's bottom with no check against window.innerHeight. A trigger near the bottom (the Chat Session actions/settings Popovers in the lower dock) opens a panel that extends below the viewport. No upward flip and no bottom clamp.
fix: In place(), compare r.bottom + panelHeight against window.innerHeight; if it would overflow, flip above the trigger (bottom: window.innerHeight - r.top + 6) or clamp top so max-height fits.

title: Tooltip.svelte is fully built but never imported anywhere
problem: Tooltip is a complete, accessible, reduced-motion-aware delayed hover/focus tooltip primitive. A repo-wide grep for Tooltip returns only the file itself — zero importers. Meanwhile 22 component/view files lean on native title= attributes for hover hints, which give inconsistent, un-styled, undelayed, and on some platforms touch-inaccessible tips.
