fix: Wire Tooltip into the components that currently fall back to title= for explanatory hints (the truncated Badge, the Composer Stop/Send buttons, the rail footer). At minimum replace the highest-value title= usages; otherwise delete it as dead code.

title: Truncated Badge loses the full value with no recoverable title
problem: Badge's header comment says the caller is expected to pass a matching title on a wrapper for the full text. But every truncate call site renders <Badge truncate>{s.model}</Badge> (Sessions/Home/Routing/Machines/Live/Agents) with NO title wrapper. A model id like us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8 clamps to 16ch and the rest is permanently unrecoverable — no hover, no expand, no copy.
fix: Make Badge self-sufficient: when truncate is set, render the text as the element's own title (capture children text or accept an explicit title prop applied to the badge span).

title: CommandPalette dialog has no focus trap — Tab escapes the modal to the page behind it
problem: The palette is the app's primary keyboard surface and declares role=dialog aria-modal=true, but unlike every other modal in this slice (Shortcuts, Sheet, Popover all use:trapFocus from $lib/actions), it never applies trapFocus. Tabbing from the input moves focus into the underlying view behind the scrim while the modal is still visually open.
fix: Add use:trapFocus to the .pal dialog div (import { trapFocus } from $lib/actions), matching Shortcuts/Sheet/Popover, so Tab/Shift+Tab cycle only within the palette.

title: CommandPalette listbox: invalid ARIA child pattern + no aria-activedescendant, so the active row isn't announced
problem: Keyboard focus stays on the search input while arrow keys move a separate active index. The rows are <button role=option> inside a <div role=listbox>, but (a) interactive buttons are not a valid listbox child, and (b) there is no aria-activedescendant on the input pointing at the active option and the options have no id. A screen-reader user arrowing hears nothing change.
fix: Give each row a stable id (id={`pal-opt-${g.index}`}), set role=combobox/aria-controls/aria-activedescendant on the input, and render the rows as non-focusable elements (div role=option, or buttons with tabindex=-1).

title: CommandPalette never scrolls the active row into view during keyboard navigation
problem: The list is height-capped (max-height: 64vh, overflow-y: auto) and the Sessions group can be long. onListKey moves active with ArrowUp/Down and clamps the index, but nothing calls scrollIntoView on the active row, so arrowing past the visible window moves selection off-screen with no scroll.
fix: Bind each row element and in onListKey after updating active (or in an $effect tracking active) call the active row's scrollIntoView({ block: nearest }). Capture refs via bind:this into an array keyed by g.index.

title: working toast kind is fully built but unreachable — store never exposes a working() method
problem: ToastKind includes working, and ToastHost.svelte renders a dedicated breathing glyph, a teal --working accent spine/glow, and a toast-breathe animation specifically for the working kind. But createToasts() only returns success/error/info convenience methods plus generic push — there is no toasts.working(...) helper, and nothing ever calls push(working, ...). All that working-toast presentation code is dead: long-running optimistic actions emit info toasts that auto-dismiss in 4.2s.
fix: Add a working: (t) => push(working, t) method (mirroring success/error/info), and wire the long-op call sites (CommandPalette.refreshFeed/pruneEmpty, etc.). If a persistent working toast is intended, let push() optionally skip the TTL timer for the working kind.

title: DetachBash capability exists through the whole stack but is never callable in the GUI
problem: DetachBash backgrounds the foreground bash command so a turn stuck on a long-running shell can be freed without killing the turn (chat/local.go DetachBash -> daemon session.detachBash -> client.DetachBash -> Bridge.DetachBash -> bridge.ts). The bridge wrapper exists but grep shows zero frontend callers. A turn blocked on a long foreground bash can only be Interrupted (which kills the whole turn) — the milder detach-and-keep-going verb is invisible.
fix: Surface a Detach bash control in Chat — next to the working indicator while store.running, or in the session menu — calling Bridge.DetachBash(sessionId) then refreshState() so the backgrounded shell appears in the shells dock. Toast on the returned boolean.

title: MemoryBackups is half-wired: the count is shown but the backup list/restore is never reachable
problem: Memory.svelte renders current.backups as a dead number (backups: 5) but never calls Bridge.MemoryBackups(scope), which lists the actual backup snapshot paths. Grep confirms MemoryBackups is UNUSED across the frontend. The user is told backups exist but given no way to list, view, or diff them from the Memory view.
fix: Wire a View backups expander in the Memory scope panel that calls Bridge.MemoryBackups(scope) and lists the paths (newest first), each opening its content for read/diff. At minimum make the count a button that reveals the list.

title: Transcript seed is O(n^2) for tool-heavy sessions because daemon State carries the full uncapped history
problem: seed() runs mapMessages over the ENTIRE State.messages array, then slices to CAP. daemon session.state() returns sess.Messages() with no cap, so a long session sends its full transcript. For every tool-role message, mapMessages does out.find(b => b.kind===tool && b.id===id) — a linear scan. With N messages and many tool calls this is O(N^2), felt as a visible hitch when a large session is opened or re-seeded on reconnect.
fix: Build a Map<toolId, ToolBlock> alongside out during the pass and look results up via the map (O(N)). Optionally slice the input messages to the last CAP*~2 before mapping.

title: Memory view should surface USER.md (auto-maintained) + a user add-on window
problem: GUI Memory currently shows profile edit only on global scope; surface the auto-maintained USER.md plus the additive user window.
fix: GUI Memory currently shows profile edit only on global scope; surface the auto-maintained USER.md plus the additive user window.

title: Settings (Config) dropdowns render as plain white input areas — must be designed
problem: Config.svelte .select uses default/native styling reading as white inputs; restyle selects to the dark token system (bg-raised, borders, brand focus, custom chevron).
fix: Config.svelte .select uses default/native styling reading as white inputs; restyle selects to the dark token system (bg-raised, borders, brand focus, custom chevron).

title: Skills + Plugins pages need an Add area (accept local path OR owner/repo GitHub)
problem: Add UI that accepts a filesystem path or a gh owner/repo, wired to the eigen skill/plugin install structure.
fix: Add UI that accepts a filesystem path or a gh owner/repo, wired to the eigen skill/plugin install structure.

title: Agent transcript rendered as one raw JSONL blob labeled json
problem: AgentTranscript returns a .jsonl file — one JSON message object per line. The view dumps it via <CodeBlock code={transcript} lang=json/>. CodeBlock does no parsing, so the rich role/content exchange is shown as an undifferentiated wall mislabeled json (it isn't valid JSON — it's JSONL). The transcript is structured enough to render as a readable conversation but is presented as a raw dump.
fix: Parse the transcript line-by-line (JSON.parse per non-empty line, tolerate bad lines) into role/content message cards in the sheet, or at minimum set lang=jsonl and pretty-print each line.

title: Agents view hides model / kind / difficulty despite its own promise and the DTO carrying them
problem: The file's header comment says it shows model/route each ran on, and BgTaskDTO/types.ts expose model, kind, difficulty, host, pid, updatedMs. The card only renders status, role, where, elapsed, tokens, attempts, escalated — model, kind, and difficulty are never displayed. For a multi-agent fan-out board the model/route a subtask ran on is core triage info.
fix: Render the available fields: add {#if t.model}<Badge>{t.model}</Badge>{/if} to the ag__top row, and surface kind/difficulty as a small meta line or in the transcript sheet header. At minimum show model since the comment commits to it.

title: Bans shown as read-only raw markdown though structured per-ban data + write API exist
problem: Memory renders banned behaviors as a single read-only <Markdown source={current.bans}/> blob. The Go memory store already parses bans into structured {Title, Rule} entries (ListBans) and can rewrite them (writeBans). The user profile right beside it is fully editable via WriteUserProfile, so the inconsistency is glaring: profile = editable, bans = an opaque text wall with no per-rule view or remove.
fix: Add a Bridge.MemoryBans(scope) returning ListBans() as a typed array and a RemoveBan(scope, title) bridge, then render bans as a list with a per-row remove. Even read-only structured rows (title + rule) would beat the current blob.

title: Dreaming diff sheet shows No differences when the diff actually errored
problem: openDiff() sets diffLoading=true then awaits ConsolidationContent + CurrentMemory. On failure it toasts the error but leaves diffPatch=empty and sets diffLoading=false. The sheet body's final {:else} branch then renders No differences — this snapshot matches the current memory. So a backend read failure (ConsolidationContent rejecting a path with os.ErrPermission, or a deleted .bak) is presented as identical / nothing changed. The toast is easy to miss behind the open sheet.
fix: Add a diffError = $state<string|null>(null) cleared at the top of openDiff and set in the catch. Render an explicit error branch in the sheet body before the empty/identical branch.

title: Rollouts timeline drops the per-session timestamp that exists on disk
problem: Rollout summary files are named 20060102-150405-{slug}.md — they carry an exact timestamp — but RawSummaries() returns only file contents, RolloutDTO has no time field, and the Rollouts strand renders each card with no when at all. Meanwhile the sibling Consolidations strand shows relTime(c.whenMs). The result is an inconsistent timeline where one strand is dated and the other timeless.
fix: Have RawSummaries (or a sibling method) return filename-derived timestamps; add whenMs int64 to RolloutDTO, parse the leading 20060102-150405 stamp like parseBakStamp, add whenMs to RolloutDTO in types.ts, and render relTime(r.whenMs) in the rollout card head.

title: GUI Routing view never surfaces vision or social capability, though both exist in the catalog and drive routing
problem: ModelDTO carries cache/context1m/reasoning/effort/search but omits Vision and Social, which ARE in llm.ModelInfo and ARE routing-relevant (TaskVision routes by m.Vision, TaskSocial by m.Social). The Routing.svelte caps() helper can only show cache/1M/reasoning/search — a vision-capable model looks identical to a text-only one. The terminal app.Models() Tags already includes vision and social, so the GUI is behind the TUI.
fix: Add Vision bool and Social bool to ModelDTO, populate from m.Vision/m.Social, add to TS RoutingDTO, and push vision/social chips in caps() in Routing.svelte.

title: Profile usage strip has no error or empty-vs-failed distinction for the log read
problem: If Bridge.ObserveSummary rejects, the catch toasts the error but summary stays null, so the Usage KPIs silently render turns=0 and errors=0 and the top-models table shows No model activity recorded yet — indistinguishable from a genuinely clean log. A transient read failure looks identical to a brand-new install. Unlike Routing/Crons/Config which keep an error state + EmptyState+Retry, Profile has no failure surface.
fix: Track a summaryError $state set in the ObserveSummary catch; render a distinct Couldn't read the usage log note with a Retry calling load(), matching the load-failure-vs-empty pattern (commit 7c6737f).

title: Routing model cards show single default effort, hiding the available effort range
problem: caps() renders effort:${m.effort} (a single default like high) or reasoning, but ModelDTO already carries effortLevels (the closed set the model accepts, e.g. low/medium/high/xhigh/max) and thinkingBudget — both present in the TS type and used by Chat.svelte's effort selector. The catalog, the one place meant to communicate model capabilities, drops this richer info.
fix: When m.reasoning and m.effortLevels?.length, render the range (a chip effort: low-max or the level count) instead of just the default; optionally show thinkingBudget. Pure presentation change in caps().

title: Routing rail lists providers with zero models, leading to a dead-end empty grid
problem: The provider rail renders every entry from data.providers including providers whose modelCount is 0 (routing.go always emits the full providerUniverse even when a provider contributes no catalog models). Clicking such a provider sets provFilter and the grid shows No models match the current filters, a confusing dead end.
fix: Either hide zero-count providers from the rail ({#each providers.filter(p => p.modelCount > 0)}) or disable/dim their button and skip the click handler when p.modelCount === 0.

title: Approve action exists but is unreachable from Live/Home — approval sessions can't be resolved without opening Chat
problem: Live sorts approval sessions to the very top with a needs approval badge, and Home's Working now zone does the same. But neither offers an Allow/Deny control — the only per-row actions on Live are Open / Interrupt / Remove. To resolve a gated approval the user must click Open, wait for Chat to load State, then approve. Bridge.Approve already exists; the command surface stops one step short.
fix: On Live (and optionally Home's live zone), for s.status === approval add an Approve affordance that opens Chat scrolled to the gate, OR fetch State + render inline Allow/Deny via Bridge.Approve.

title: Home/Ideas feed items with only a URL still force-start a session via StartFromFeed
problem: actOn(it) always calls Bridge.StartFromFeed(it.dir, it.task) and routes to chat. For a github feed item that is fundamentally a link (it.url set), Start spins up a new session and submits it.task even when the meaningful action is to open the URL. StartFromFeed creates the session first and only then sends task — if task is empty it leaves a brand-new empty session behind every time the user clicks Start on a link-only item.
fix: In actOn / the card foot, when it.task is empty, hide or disable Start (only show Open); or have the button behavior key off whether a task exists. Guard StartFromFeed callers so a task-less item routes to openURL instead of creating an orphan session.

title: Live polls sessions.refresh() every 2s unconditionally — keeps running while daemon offline and window hidden
problem: The polling $effect schedules tick() -> sessions.refresh() every 2s for as long as the view is mounted, with no awareness of daemon.status or document visibility. When the daemon is offline every tick is a guaranteed-failing RPC; when the window is backgrounded it still hammers the daemon. Live has no online reference at all, unlike Chat which gates on daemon.status.
fix: Gate the poll on daemon.status === online (skip the refresh, keep the timer cheap, or rely on daemon.onReconnect to resume) and pause on document.hidden via a visibilitychange listener, resuming on focus.

title: SteerInput silent fallback can drop a message into a new turn the user didn't intend
problem: send(): when store.running, it calls SteerInput; if steered returns false it silently falls back to SendInput(...) which queues a fresh turn. The user typed mid-turn expecting to steer; if steering isn't accepted, the text instead becomes a new queued turn with no indication. The success path toasts steered into the running turn, but the fallback path is silent.
fix: When the steer fails and you fall back to SendInput, toast something like couldn't steer — queued as a new turn so the behavior change is visible. Or surface a confirm before queueing.

title: Composer comment claims MAX_ROWS is read from a CSS custom property, but it is a JS const
problem: The grow() comment states The cap is read from a CSS custom property so the JS and the stylesheet never disagree, but MAX_ROWS = 8 is a plain JS constant and the stylesheet has no corresponding --max-rows token. The exact invariant the comment promises does not exist.
fix: Either define --composer-max-rows: 8 in the component CSS and have grow() read it via getComputedStyle, or correct the comment to state the cap is a JS constant.

title: EmptyState glyph is decorative-only with no semantic mapping to its meaning
problem: The glyph is rendered aria-hidden=true (correct), but the whole EmptyState block has no landmark/role and its <h2> heading appears at an arbitrary level regardless of context. An EmptyState shown as a top-level view body and one shown inside a card both hard-code <h2>, which can produce skipped/duplicated heading levels for screen-reader users.
fix: Either accept a headingLevel prop (default 2) so callers nesting an EmptyState inside a section can set the correct level, or render the title as a non-heading element with role=heading aria-level driven by context.

title: ToastHost reduceMotion is captured once at module load and never reacts to OS changes
problem: reduceMotion is a top-level const evaluating matchMedia(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).matches exactly once, and enterMs/exitMs derive from it once. Every other component honors reduced-motion via CSS @media that re-evaluates live, but here a user who toggles the OS setting after first evaluation keeps the original animated/stilled toast transitions until reload.
fix: Make it reactive: store the MediaQueryList, subscribe to its change event (in an $effect with teardown), and update a $state flag so enterMs/exitMs recompute.

title: App.svelte route-change fly transition ignores prefers-reduced-motion
problem: The outlet page uses in:fly={{ y: 6, duration: 180 }} on every route change. As ToastHost documents, Svelte JS transitions do NOT honor prefers-reduced-motion on their own — they must gate duration to 0 manually. App.svelte does no such gating, so the shell's primary navigation animation plays for users who asked to reduce motion.
fix: Compute a reduceMotion flag (matchMedia(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).matches) and use duration: reduceMotion ? 0 : 180 in the in:fly options.

title: CommandPalette and Shortcuts overlays can stack, and a single Escape is handled by both
problem: Both components register independent window keydown listeners. Opening Shortcuts with ? then pressing Ctrl/Cmd+K opens the palette on top of it (z-index 81 vs 91, so Shortcuts renders ABOVE the palette while the palette grabs focus), and a single Escape fires both onWinKey (palette hide) and onKey (shortcuts close). No single overlay-coordination layer.
fix: Coordinate the two global overlays — a tiny shared overlay store (or have each opener close the other), and align z-indexes so the focused/most-recent overlay is on top. At minimum have the palette's show() dismiss the Shortcuts sheet and vice-versa.

title: Shortcuts cheatsheet is incomplete/misleading — duplicate Enter rows, omits palette actions and toast dismissal
problem: The cheatsheet is the discovery surface for the keyboard-first audience, but its rows table has two separate Enter entries (Open selected item and Send a message (Chat)) that read as a contradiction, and it never mentions the concrete palette verbs that exist (Start a session / Prune empty / Refresh feed) nor that the palette also fuzzy-jumps to any session.
fix: Group the two Enter rows under a context column or merge with clearer scoping (Enter — open selection / send message in Chat), and add a short line pointing at the palette's verb set.

title: TopBar title shows the raw route id, never the session/context — chat instead of the session title
problem: The masthead h1 renders {router.route} verbatim (CSS capitalize), so on the Chat route it always reads Chat even when router.param identifies a specific session whose title is known via the sessions store. The top bar can never tell you which session you're in. The Rail/CommandPalette already know session titles; the bar throws that context away.
fix: Derive a display title: when route===chat and router.param is set, look up sessions.list for that id and show its title (fallback to Chat); otherwise keep the capitalized route. Optionally centralize a route->label map in router.svelte.ts.

title: Bridge.Stats() is unused and typed Promise<unknown> while a typed DaemonStats already exists
problem: Live daemon stats are delivered via the eigen:daemon:stats event stream, so the request/response Bridge.Stats() RPC is never called from the frontend. It is also the only bridge wrapper returning unknown, even though types.ts DaemonStats is the exact shape the Go Stats() returns.
fix: If kept (for an explicit refresh), type it as Promise<DaemonStats | null>; if redundant with the push stream, remove the wrapper to keep bridge.ts a 1:1 surface.

title: FeedFor (per-project feed drill-in) is bridged but never used
problem: Bridge.FeedFor(dir) returns the proactive-feed items scoped to a single project dir, implemented server-side in feed.go, but grep shows it is UNUSED. Home renders only the global feed lanes; there is no per-project drill-in even though Home rows and sessions carry a dir.
fix: Either wire FeedFor into a per-project view (expanding a Home project row, or a project header in Sessions/Chat to show that dir's loose ends) or drop the FeedFor wrapper from bridge.ts and the Go method.

title: SessionInfoDTO.updated is unix-nano but undocumented at the seam, and the nano->ms rel() is copy-pasted across three views
problem: daemon SessionInfo.Updated is unix-nano, passed straight through dto.go. Home/Sessions/Live each independently divide by 1e6 in their own rel() helper, but types.ts gives updated no unit comment. Any new consumer that treats updated as millis will compute timestamps ~10^6x off, and the triplicated rel() invites exactly that drift.
fix: Comment updated as // unix nano in types.ts, and extract the shared nano->relative-time helper into lib (a relTime() in status.ts or a time util) so the three views call one source of truth.

title: types.ts DaemonStats drifts from dto.go: daemon identity/version fields are missing
problem: internal/daemon/protocol.go DaemonStats emits version, executable, binary_sha256, vcs_revision, vcs_modified every second, but the frontend DaemonStats type omits all of them. The Observe runtime panel shows uptime/goroutines/heap/rss/gc/go_version but cannot show the eigen version or which binary/revision is hosting sessions because the type lacks the fields.
fix: Add version?, executable?, binary_sha256?, vcs_revision?, vcs_modified? to the DaemonStats type and surface version (and optionally short binary_sha256/vcs_revision) in the Observe runtime panel.

title: Memory Add note never focuses the textarea it reveals
problem: Clicking Add note (or Add the first note in the empty state) toggles composing=true and renders the compose textarea, but focus stays on the button. The user has to click into the textarea before typing — extra step on the primary capture action. Chat autofocuses its title/goal inputs.
fix: Bind the textarea and focus it when composing flips true (an $effect that focuses on mount, or use:autofocus action).

title: Memory note + profile textareas have no keyboard submit, unlike Chat's goal editor
problem: Chat.svelte commits its multi-line goal editor on Cmd/Ctrl+Enter. Memory's Add note textarea and the profile editor offer no keyboard submit at all — the user must mouse to the Save button every time. For a quick-capture surface this is friction and an inconsistency.
fix: Add onkeydown={(e) => { if (e.key === Enter && (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey)) { e.preventDefault(); saveNote(); } }} to the note textarea (and saveProfile to the profile textarea).

title: Memory shows No memory yet empty state even when backups/consolidations exist
problem: The empty-state guard checks noteCount/adHoc/summary/bans/profile but ignores current.backups. A scope consolidated down to nothing-currently-injected (notes pruned, summary empty) but still has backup snapshots renders No {scope} memory yet even though the Dreaming view would show consolidation history for it.
fix: Include backups in the non-empty test, or change the empty-state copy/CTA to acknowledge prior history (link to Dreaming) when current.backups > 0.

title: Skills paging reveals in discovery order then re-buckets, so Show more injects cards into earlier shelves
problem: visibleActive = filtered.slice(0, activeShown) takes the first N skills in discovery order, then shelves groups them user->project->extra. Because slicing happens before grouping, clicking Show N more can append skills to ANY shelf — including Yours above the fold — rather than progressively filling shelves top-to-bottom. The reveal is visually jumpy.
fix: Page after grouping rather than before: compute full shelves from all filtered skills, then cap the total rendered cards across shelves in order (or cap per shelf).

title: role=tab segmented controls lack arrow-key navigation and tabpanel association
problem: All three views mark their segmented switches as role=tablist / role=tab with aria-selected, but implement none of the ARIA tab keyboard contract: no roving tabindex, no ArrowLeft/ArrowRight, no role=tabpanel / aria-controls. Screen-reader users hear tab semantics but get plain-button behavior, and keyboard users can't arrow between segments.
fix: Either add the standard tab keyboard pattern (roving tabindex + Arrow handler, aria-controls pointing at a role=tabpanel) or downgrade to role=group with aria-pressed toggle buttons so announced semantics match behavior.

title: splitNotes keeps the leading heading its comment claims to drop, so the first note card is a stray # Memory line
problem: The doc comment states splitNotes drops empties and a leading top-level heading, but the implementation only TrimSpaces and splits on blank lines — it never strips a leading # heading. If MEMORY.md begins with a top-level heading followed by a blank line, the Notes section renders a card whose entire content is just # Memory.
fix: Either implement the documented behavior (skip a first chunk that is solely a top-level ATX heading) or fix the comment. Implementing the drop is better since the heading is structural.

title: Config select shows no saving feedback while a commit is in flight
problem: Boolean toggles, selects, and chips all disable themselves via disabled={saving[f.key]} during commit, but there's no visible progress indicator — the control just goes inert. On a slow SetConfig the user gets no signal the change is being persisted, and for a select the value appears applied instantly while the daemon may still reject and revert it via the catch.
fix: Add a lightweight inline saving... affordance per row when saving[f.key] is true (a small spinner or dimmed label next to the control).

title: Crons crontab decoder ignores day-of-month/month combos and silently shows raw spec
problem: cadence() decodes common patterns but several real specs fall through to the raw glob: e.g. */15 9-17 * * 1-5 (ranges/steps), 0 0 1 * * with mon!=* combos, and the every Nh branch only matches when mi===0 AND mon===* AND dow===*. The crontab column is sold as reads as a rhythm not a glob but degrades to the unparsed five-field string for fairly ordinary schedules, and shows the spec twice.
fix: Either expand cadence() to handle ranges/lists/steps in dow/month and day-of-month, or when it can't decode still label it (custom schedule) above the verbatim spec rather than duplicating the raw glob as both cadence and spec.

title: Plugins exposes rich DTO fields (installedMs, scanCount, marketplace owner/addedMs) that no row renders
problem: InstalledPluginDTO carries InstalledMs and ScanCount, and MarketplaceDTO carries Owner and AddedMs, all populated by plugins.go, but the view renders none of them. A plugin row never shows when it was installed or how many files were scanned, and a marketplace never shows its owner or when it was added. These are cheap trust/provenance signals the backend already computes.
fix: Show install date (rel-time from installedMs) and scanCount next to the scan badge on plugin rows, and owner/added on marketplace rows — small additions reusing the existing rel-time formatter.

title: Chat near-limit compact nudge and Compact menu item give no feedback while compacting
problem: The dock near context limit — compact? button disables on compacting || running but shows no in-progress affordance — Compact can take seconds. The user clicks, the label stays the same just greyed, no spinner/compacting.... The menu Compact item has the same gap. Compare addDir/exportSession which use Button loading={...}.
fix: Swap the nudge label to compacting... (or add a spinner) while compacting, and on the menu Compact item show loading state. The compacting $state already exists.

title: Home relative-time labels are frozen — not tied to the shared clock like Live/Sessions
problem: Home's rel(updatedNano) computes Date.now() at render time but reads no reactive clock, while Live (void now.ms) and Sessions deliberately tie rel() to the shared 1Hz now store so labels tick. On Home the Resume rows 5m ago labels go stale and only update when sessions.refresh() happens to re-render them.
fix: Import { now } from $lib/stores/clock.svelte and add void now.ms; at the top of Home's rel() (matching Live/Sessions).

title: Home stat strip and live/resume rows are <button>s with no accessible name
problem: The cockpit strip button has title=Open Observe but its content is purely numbers/labels, so screen readers announce sessions 0 running with no action context. The Working now (.lr) and Resume (.row) rows are buttons whose accessible name is the concatenation of inner spans only — no aria-label tying it to the open session X action.
fix: Add aria-label to the strip button (Open Observe — telemetry) and to .lr/.row buttons (aria-label={`Open session ${s.title || untitled}`}). title alone is insufficient for accname.

title: Sessions Export has no daemon-offline / disabled guard while other actions implicitly require online
problem: Sessions never references daemon.status. Resume routes to Chat (fine offline-ish), but Export/Delete/Prune all hit the daemon and throw -> error toast when offline, with the buttons fully enabled and inviting. Chat gates the composer on online; Sessions/Live offer no such signal, so on a dropped daemon the archive looks fully operational and every action fails with a raw error toast.
fix: Either disable mutating buttons (Export/Delete/Prune) when daemon.status !== online with a title explaining why, or show a thin offline banner on these views, matching Chat's online gating.

title: bg_done wake event spuriously flips an idle session to "working" forever
problem: The daemon emits a `bg_done` WireEvent (eventKindName → "bg_done", protocol.go:185) as the wake signal when a background task finishes; it flows through pump.go → WireEventDTO to the frontend listener. In start(), the running-flag guard is `if (!m.replay && m.event.kind !== "done") running = true;` — `bg_done` is neither a replay nor "done", so it sets running=true. But onEvent()'s switch has NO case for "bg_done", so nothing ever clears it and no real turn is in flight. The session then shows the "working…" indicator and a Stop button indefinitely, the composer blocks normal send into steer mode, and Compact/Clear/Resend stay disabled — all because a background agent (unrelated to this chat turn) reported completion.
fix: In start(), make the running guard explicit about which kinds imply an in-flight turn rather than excluding only "done": e.g. `const liveKinds = m.event.kind === "text" || m.event.kind === "reasoning" || m.event.kind === "tool_start" || m.event.kind === "tool_result" || m.event.kind === "note" || m.event.kind === "approval"; if (!m.replay && liveKinds) running = true;`. Alternatively add `case "bg_done": break;` in onEvent and exclude it from the running guard. The note sibling already handles any display.

title: Reconnect re-attach re-seeds history and drops the in-flight live block, duplicating streamed content
problem: attach() is called both on first mount and again on every daemon.onReconnect(). On reconnect it calls Bridge.Subscribe(id) (daemon replays its event buffer through the same listener registered once in t.start()) AND Bridge.State(id).then(s => t.seed(s.messages, s.running)). seed() does `history = mapMessages(...)` — a full replacement — and resets running from the snapshot. But it never clears `live`: if a turn was mid-stream when the connection dropped, the half-streamed assistant text sits in `live` and is now orphaned (the seeded snapshot also contains that same partial/complete text as history). The replay buffer then re-feeds text deltas that get appended to a fresh live block, so the user sees the in-flight reply twice (once stale in the dangling live block region, once rebuilt). The comment claims re-seed is "safe to call again on reconnect" but it does not reconcile the live block or pending coalescer state.
fix: Have transcript expose a reseed path that resets the live/pending coalescer before replacing history: in seed(), also `live = null; resetPending(); if (rafId) { cancelAnimationFrame(rafId); rafId = 0; }`. (resetPending/rafId are module-private today — either move the reset into seed() or add a `t.reset()` the view calls before re-seed.) This makes the daemon replay rebuild the live block from a clean slate instead of appending onto a stale one.

title: Live token counts stream in every event but the context-usage ring never updates until the turn ends
problem: WireEvent carries InTokens/OutTokens (protocol.go:150-151), they survive the full seam (gui/dto.go:60-61 → types.ts WireEventDTO.inTokens/outTokens), but transcript.onEvent() reads only kind/text/tool fields and discards inTokens/outTokens entirely. The dock context ring (pct, nearLimit) is driven solely by sess.tokens, which is refreshed only via refreshState() — fired on `store.running === false` (turn end) or approvalSeq bumps. So during a long streaming turn the context % and the "near context limit — compact?" nudge are frozen at the pre-turn value, then jump at the end. For a turn that itself pushes context over the limit, the user gets no live warning while it's happening.
fix: Track the latest streamed token counts in the transcript (e.g. a `liveTokens = $state(0)` updated in onEvent from e.outTokens/e.inTokens when present) and expose a getter; have Chat.svelte fold it into the displayed token count while running, or at minimum bump a small reactive signal the dock reads so the ring animates during the turn. Even just surfacing outTokens growth gives live feedback.

title: mapMessages matches tool results by id with a global out.find, collapsing repeated tool ids across steps into the wrong block
problem: In mapMessages (line 202), a `tool` role message looks up its call via `out.find(b => b.kind === "tool" && b.id === id)` across the ENTIRE accumulated output, not the most recent step. Tool-call ids are unique per call in well-behaved providers, but importer/foreign-transcript paths (Codex/Claude) and any provider that reuses or blanks ids (toolCallId ?? "" yields "" for missing ids) will collide: every tool message with an empty/duplicate id attaches its result to the FIRST matching block, leaving later calls perpetually `done:false` (spinner stuck) and overwriting an earlier result. The live onEvent tool_result path correctly scans `history` from the end backward (line 113); the seed path does not match that semantics. (Related to GUI-018, which covers the O(n^2) perf of the same mapMessages scan; this entry is the correctness defect — wrong-block attachment + empty-id collision.)
fix: Mirror the live path: search backward for the most recent undone tool block with the id, and skip matching when id is empty (`if (id) { for (let i = out.length-1; i>=0; i--) ... }` else push a standalone result block). This also resolves the O(n^2) concern but the correctness fix (last-match + empty-id guard) is the load-bearing part.

title: Steer-vs-send race: store.running can be stale at click time, sending a fresh turn the user meant to steer (or vice versa)
problem: send() branches on `store?.running` to choose SteerInput vs SendInput. `running` is local reactive state derived from the stream (flips false on the `done` event / State seed). Between the `done` event arriving and the user pressing Enter, or in the window where a new turn started server-side but the first delta hasn't flipped running=true yet, the client's view of running is stale. If running is false but a turn just (re)started, SendInput queues a second concurrent turn; if running is true but the turn already finished, SteerInput is attempted on an idle session and silently falls back to SendInput (line 132). The fallback is silent — the ledger flags the missing toast (GUI-034) but the underlying decision being made on a racy local flag is the deeper correctness issue. (Related to GUI-034, which covers the silent fallback toast; this is the deeper racy-flag decision.)
fix: Don't decide steer-vs-send purely on the local running flag. Prefer a backend op that injects-if-running and reports what it did (SteerInput already returns a bool); on idle just call it and let the daemon resolve, or have send() always attempt SteerInput first when there's any chance of a running turn and treat the returned bool authoritatively (true=steered, false=send a fresh turn) instead of pre-branching on store.running.

title: No loading state on session switch: stale transcript flashes before the new session's State() resolves
problem: The lifecycle $effect sets `store = t` synchronously after attach(), but seed() only populates history once Bridge.State(id) resolves (async). Until then `store.history` is [] and `store.live` is null, so `isEmpty` (sess != null && messages.length === 0) is FALSE (sess is still the previous session's snapshot or null) and the transcript region renders an empty VirtualList — no skeleton, no spinner. When switching from session A to session B, the dock also still shows A's model/tokens/goal/pending until State resolves because `sess` is only nulled when id is empty/missing, not on every id change. So a session switch briefly shows B's empty body with A's dock metadata. GUI-006 covers the first-load no-loading-state; the cross-session stale-dock bleed is additional.
fix: Null `sess` at the top of the lifecycle $effect when the id changes (before attach), and add a loading flag set true before attach() and cleared in the State().then/catch. Render a transcript skeleton when loading && history.length === 0, and don't render dock values from a previous session's sess while loading the next.

title: approvalSeq only ever increments, so a re-attach/replay that re-delivers an old approval event re-fires refreshState but a resolved-then-replayed approval can resurrect a stale gate
problem: On the `approval` event, approvalSeq++ unconditionally — including for REPLAYED events (the start() guard only special-cases running for replay, onEvent runs for replay events too). On reconnect, the daemon replays its buffer, so a previously-resolved approval event in the buffer bumps approvalSeq again, triggering refreshState(). That refetch is the safety net, but the gate UI (sess.pending) is then whatever State returns — if the daemon's pendingList still lists it (the known APP-036 double-emit) the Allow/Deny dock reappears for an approval the user may have already actioned in a prior connection. The frontend has no dedup of approval ids it has already resolved.
fix: Bump approvalSeq only for live (non-replay) approval events, OR have approve() record resolved approval ids and filter them out of the rendered sess.pending list until the next authoritative State that no longer contains them. Pairs with the daemon-side dedup (APP-036).

title: Empty-session starter chips and dock starters are gated on `online` but not on `missing`/loading, and send() swallows the gate
problem: The starter state (isEmpty) renders chips with `disabled={!online}`, but isEmpty is `sess != null && sess.messages.length === 0 && !store?.running`. During the async gap after switching to an empty session, sess can be the PRIOR session's non-empty snapshot, so isEmpty is false and the warm starter never shows for a genuinely empty new session until State resolves; conversely after Clear, sess.messages is emptied by refreshState but store.history may still hold the just-cleared blocks for a frame. The starter and transcript can briefly both be wrong. Also, clicking a starter chip calls send(s) which, if store.running raced true, would steer the canned prompt into a running turn instead of starting fresh.
fix: Derive isEmpty from the authoritative loaded state for the CURRENT id (guard with the loading flag and reset sess on id change per the loading-state fix), and have the starter chips call a send path that forces a fresh turn (never steer) since they are only meant to appear on an idle empty session.

title: Composer Enter-to-send has no keyboard escape hatch and the disabled-reason is only conveyed visually/title
problem: When disabled (daemon offline), the textarea placeholder and footer hint show the reason, but the textarea has no aria-describedby tying the disabledReason to the field, and the footer is aria-hidden="true" so AT never hears the char count or the offline reason. The Send button's accessible state changes (enabled/disabled) but the reason text is title-only, which is not reliably announced. There's also no aria-disabled messaging when running blocks send.
fix: Give the textarea aria-describedby pointing at a (non-aria-hidden) status element carrying the disabledReason when disabled, and surface the offline reason via the Button's accessible name rather than only title. Drop aria-hidden from the hint or duplicate the essential state into an sr-only live region.

title: Stop/Interrupt gives no in-flight feedback and can be clicked repeatedly with no debounce
problem: interrupt() calls Bridge.Interrupt with no loading/disabled state on the Stop button and no optimistic indication. On a slow daemon the user can mash Stop, firing multiple Interrupt RPCs; the daemon-side interrupt also reports success even when nothing was interrupted (APP-037), so the user gets no signal whether the interrupt took. The button stays "Stop" until the `done` event clears running, with no "stopping…" transitional state.
fix: Add an `interrupting` $state set on click and cleared on the next done/running=false (or RPC resolve), disable the Stop button while interrupting, and toast/label "stopping…". Optionally surface the daemon's interrupted-or-not result once APP-037 lands.

title: rows derived array spreads the full history on every live-block frame during streaming
problem: `rows = $derived.by(() => live ? [...h, live] : h)` allocates a new array spreading the entire history (up to CAP=2000 blocks) on EVERY live-block update — and the live block is reassigned (`live = {...live, text: ...}`) once per animation frame while streaming. So a long streaming turn re-spreads a 2000-element array ~60×/sec, which then re-derives VirtualList's indexByKey/offsets. The transcript was carefully built to mutate history in place to avoid copies, but this view-level spread reintroduces an O(history) allocation per frame.
fix: Avoid spreading the whole history every frame: render history and the live block as two adjacent lists (history via VirtualList, the single live block appended outside the windowed list or as a fixed trailing row), or feed VirtualList a stable items reference and pass `live` separately. At minimum memoize so the spread only happens when history length changes, appending live by index without re-copying h.

title: Tool results are stored and rendered fully uncapped — a large bash/read result floods CodeBlock with megabytes of DOM + regex
problem: ToolBlock.result is held in full (no length cap anywhere in the store), and ToolCallCard passes block.result straight into CodeBlock. CodeBlock runs two global regex passes (COMMENT/STRING splitter + KEYWORDS + NUMBER) over the ENTIRE string in the `html` $derived and then injects the whole result via {@html}. A bash command or a `read` of a large file (commonly tens of thousands of lines / multiple MB) builds a giant escaped+span-wrapped HTML string and mounts it all in the DOM. CSS max-height:360px only visually clips — every line is still parsed and in the DOM. With several such cards expanded this is a major jank/memory hit. DiffView caps at 400/PREVIEW=120 lines but CodeBlock has no equivalent.
fix: Cap the rendered code in CodeBlock: if code exceeds a threshold (e.g. > ~2000 lines or ~200KB), tint+render only a head slice and show a 'show full (N more lines)' toggle mirroring DiffView's PREVIEW/expand pattern, keeping the full text only for the copy button. Alternatively cap result length at the store seam (truncate with an explicit '… (truncated, M bytes)' marker) so tinting never runs over megabytes.

title: Synthesized edit/write diff emits a spurious deletion (or addition) line and wrong hunk counts for empty old_string / empty content
problem: splitLines("") returns [""] (length 1), not []. So an `edit` whose old_string is empty (a pure insertion — valid for several edit tools) produces `@@ -1,1 +1,N @@` with a leading `-` empty deletion line, and DiffView's diffstat then reports a phantom '1 deletion'. The mirror case (empty new_string, or write with empty content) emits a spurious `+` empty addition. The hunk header line counts are also wrong (claims 1 line where there are 0). This misrepresents what the tool actually changed.
fix: Make splitLines return [] for an empty string (e.g. `if (s === '') return []; return s.replace(/\n$/,'').split('\n');`), and compute hunk counts from the resulting array lengths. editHunk/writeDiff already iterate the arrays, so empty input then emits no spurious change line and the @@ counts and diffstat become correct.

title: DiffView renders code as a real data <table> with no presentation role, and add/del status is conveyed only by color + aria-hidden sign gutter
problem: The diff body is a semantic <table><tr><td> with no role override, so screen readers announce it as a data table ('table, N rows, 2 columns', cell-by-cell navigation) for what is actually preformatted code. Worse, the only signal of whether a line was added or deleted is (a) the background color and (b) the +/− sign — and the sign lives in a <td class="diff__gutter" aria-hidden="true">. So a screen-reader user hears every add and del line as undifferentiated text with no indication of which is which.
fix: Add role="presentation" (or role="none") to the <table> (and tr/td) so it isn't announced as tabular data, OR keep the table but expose the change kind non-visually: drop aria-hidden on the gutter for add/del rows, or add a visually-hidden prefix per line (e.g. an sr-only span reading 'added'/'removed' on .diff__row--add/--del .diff__line). Context/hunk/meta rows can stay aria-hidden on their gutter.

title: ToolCallCard glyph reads green 'ok' for a done-without-result tool while the dot + note correctly read amber 'warn'
problem: dotState is computed as block.done && !block.result ? 'warn' : 'ok' (amber), and the body shows an amber 'result not received' note — the comment at lines 19-33 explicitly says these must agree. But `tone` (which colors the header glyph via tool__glyph--{tone}) is `block.isError ? 'error' : block.done ? 'ok' : 'running'`, so the same indeterminate case gets tone='ok' → a green glyph. The header therefore signals success (green glyph) while the dot and note signal warning (amber) for the identical state, contradicting the stated intent.
fix: Derive `tone` from the same three-way logic as dotState: error → error; done && !result → 'warn'; done && result → 'ok'; else 'running'. Add a .tool__glyph--warn { color: var(--warn); } rule so the glyph agrees with the dot and the amber note.

title: Multi-line raw HTML block from the model is collapsed onto one line (newlines lost) in Markdown
problem:
marked emits a multi-line raw HTML block (e.g. a pasted `
\n x\n
`) as a SINGLE `html` token whose .text is the full multi-line raw string (verified against marked 18.0.5). The component renders it as `

{html.text}

`. Because it's a normal flow

, the embedded newlines collapse to single spaces, so the literal HTML the model wrote is shown mangled on one line. Safe (escaped) but garbled — and a single-line `` is shown as visible comment text.
fix:
Render raw HTML-block text in a whitespace-preserving element instead of a

: e.g. `

{text}
` (or a div with white-space: pre-wrap), so multi-line literal HTML reads as written. Optionally skip emitting anything for tokens whose trimmed text is purely an HTML comment.

title: DiffView recomputes additions/deletions/hunks by filtering the full rows array three separate times on every reactive pass
problem: additions, deletions, and hunks are three independent $derived that each call rows.filter(...) over the entire parsed rows array. For a large diff (the LARGE=400+ case this component explicitly targets) that's three extra full passes over potentially thousands of rows on top of the parse pass, recomputed whenever `patch` or `expanded` changes. The parse loop already classifies every line and could tally these counts in one pass.
fix: Tally counts inside the single `rows` $derived parse loop (increment local add/del/hunk counters as each Row is classified) and return them alongside rows (e.g. return { rows, additions, deletions, hunks }), or compute all three in one $derived.by that loops once. Removes two full O(n) passes.

title: CodeBlock copy button has CSS transitions but no prefers-reduced-motion guard, unlike every sibling rich component
problem: `.code__copy` animates background and color via `transition` (line 121), but CodeBlock is the only one of the five rich components with no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block — Markdown, ToolCallCard, DiffView, and StatusDot all gate their transitions/animations. The codebase's own pattern treats even a lone color transition (Markdown's .md-link) as motion worth gating, so CodeBlock is an outlier that ignores the user's reduced-motion preference.
fix: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .code__copy { transition: none; } }` at the end of CodeBlock's <style>, matching the pattern in DiffView/ToolCallCard/Markdown.

title: CodeBlock copy button gives no feedback when the result is empty and silently no-ops when clipboard is denied
problem: copy() awaits navigator.clipboard.writeText(code) and on rejection swallows the error with no user feedback (the comment says 'leave the label unchanged rather than lie'), so a denied/unavailable clipboard looks like a dead button. Separately, the copy control is always shown even when `code` is empty string (e.g. a tool with an empty result still renders a CodeBlock in some branches), letting the user 'copy' nothing with a success checkmark.
fix: On the catch branch, surface a brief failed state (e.g. set a `failed` flag that shows '✕ copy failed' for ~1.4s) so the click isn't silent; and/or disable/hide the copy button when code.trim() is empty so the success checkmark never lies about copying nothing.

title: Collapsed large DiffView cannot horizontally scroll its preview because the clipped container sets overflow:hidden
problem: While a large diff is collapsed, .diff__scroll gets .diff__scroll--clipped { overflow: hidden }, which kills BOTH axes. The preview body uses white-space: pre (no wrap), so any line wider than the container is truncated with no way to scroll right until the user expands the whole diff. The intent was only to clip vertical overflow for the fade affordance.
fix: Use overflow-x:auto; overflow-y:hidden in .diff__scroll--clipped (clip only the vertical for the fade) so wide preview lines remain horizontally scrollable while collapsed.

title: Remote-dial error gives no retry — a transient ssh failure forces close+reopen to try again
problem: drill() dials over ssh, which the file header itself calls a SLOW call that may fail (host briefly offline, dropped VPN, ssh timeout). On failure the sheet shows a static 'Couldn't reach this host' block with only an install hint — there is NO Retry control. The user must close the slide-over and re-click the card to attempt the dial again. Every other load path in this view and the wider GUI (the machines-list error EmptyState at line 103-107, Profile/Sessions per commit 7c6737f) pairs a failure with a Retry button. This one omits it, so a flaky-network ssh dial is a dead end inside the sheet.
fix: Add a Retry control to the remoteError branch that re-invokes the dial for the currently-open machine, e.g. {#snippet}/inline <Button variant="secondary" onclick={() => openMachine && drill(openMachine)}>Retry</Button>. drill() already takes a MachineDTO and bumps remoteSeq, so re-calling it with openMachine is safe and reuses the existing alive-guard.

title: Remote sessions are display-only dead ends — you cannot open/attach a remote session from the drill-in
problem: The drill-in dials ssh, lists live remote sessions (title, status, dir, model, turns) and renders each as a static .rrow div. There is no onclick, no Open/Attach affordance, and no route to a remote chat. The whole point of seeing a remote's live sessions — including ones in 'approval' or 'working' status (the row even pulses for those) — is to act on them, yet the rows do nothing. eigen supports remote attach end-to-end (internal/remote/dial.go Dial returns a daemon.Client; the TUI has remoteAttachLoop), so the capability exists but the GUI surfaces it as a read-only table. A user staring at a pulsing 'approval' dot on a remote session has no way to resolve it.
fix: Make each .rrow interactive (role=button + keydown, or wrap in the existing interactive Card pattern) and, on activate, route to a remote chat view keyed by (machine.ssh, session.id) — mirroring the TUI's remote attach. Minimum viable: add an explicit Open/Attach button per row that calls a new Bridge.AttachRemote(ssh, id) (or opens Chat with a remote target param). If full remote attach is out of scope for this pass, at least make the pulsing-approval rows actionable or drop the misleading pulse.

title: RemoteSessions can block ~30s per dial with no cancel, and a closed sheet never aborts the ssh subprocess
problem: Dial() spawns `ssh ... eigen daemon stdio` with SSHArgs that set only ServerAliveInterval — NO ConnectTimeout. cmd.Start() returns immediately, then c.List() waits up to the daemon request timeout (EIGEN_DAEMON_TIMEOUT or 30s, see client.go:163) for a protocol reply. So a wedged/slow host pins the call ~30s. RemoteSessions takes no context.Context, so closeDrill() (which only bumps remoteSeq and resets UI state) cannot cancel the in-flight bridge call — the ssh subprocess keeps running server-side until List() times out and ListSessions closes it. Rapidly opening/closing or dialing several machines leaks transient ssh processes for up to 30s each, and the UI just discards the late result.
fix: Two parts: (1) add `-o ConnectTimeout=10` to SSHArgs (or a tighter, separate base for the read-only ListSessions peek) so an unreachable host fails fast instead of riding the 30s daemon timeout; (2) thread a context through — give Bridge.RemoteSessions a ctx (Wails supports context-bound bound methods) or expose a CancelRemoteDial, and have closeDrill cancel it so the ssh process is torn down immediately when the user dismisses the sheet.

title: Card #each key is m.name, but saved + ssh-config can collide / be blank, risking duplicate-key and mis-render
file: internal/gui/frontend/src/views/Machines.svelte:117 ({#each machines as m (m.name)}) vs internal/remote/hosts.go:125-155 (Machines merge)
problem: The list keys on m.name. Machines() in hosts.go merges hosts.json and ssh_config into a map keyed by name, so within one snapshot names are unique — but the name can be empty: a hosts.json entry whose JSON key is "" (or a saved Host with no SSH falls back to ssh=name="") produces a Machine with Name=="". If two such malformed entries exist they cannot collide in the Go map (same key overwrites) but a single empty-named machine renders a card whose key is "" and whose .mc__name is blank — silently unlabeled. There's no guard or fallback label. Keys also being the display label means a rename in hosts.json reshuffles DOM identity unnecessarily.
fix: Key on a stable composite that can't be empty, e.g. (m.name || m.ssh) or `${m.name}|${m.ssh}`. In the card, fall back the displayed name to m.ssh when m.name is empty so a malformed/blank entry is still identifiable. Optionally have remote.Machines() skip or warn on empty-named hosts.json keys.

title: Machines list never refreshes after a CLI `remote add` / ssh_config edit — load() runs once on mount
problem: The header explicitly tells users to add hosts via `eigen remote add` or by editing ~/.ssh/config, promising 'detected entries show up here automatically.' But the view loads once in the mount $effect and never re-reads. There is no eigen:machines event (confirmed: none emitted anywhere in internal/gui), no window-focus / route-re-entry refresh, and no manual Refresh button when the list is non-empty (Retry only exists in the error EmptyState). So after the user follows the very instruction in the header, the new host does not appear until the whole GUI is relaunched or they navigate away and back in a way that remounts. The 'automatically' copy is therefore false.
fix: Add a lightweight refresh: re-call load() on window focus / route re-entry (matching the pattern suggested for Skills in APP-039), and/or add a small Refresh affordance in the header next to the count. Machines() is instant + local (no ssh), so refreshing freely is cheap. If a daemon-side signal is feasible, emit eigen:machines after remote add and subscribe.

title: Bridge.Machines() is typed MachinesDTO|null but a null resolution masquerades as 'No machines yet'
problem: bridge.ts declares Machines(): Promise<MachinesDTO|null>. In load(), `if (seq === loadSeq) data = d` assigns d directly, so data can become null without an exception. With loading=false and error=null, the template falls through every guard to the `machines.length === 0` branch and renders the 'No machines yet — add a remote…' empty state. A null payload (e.g. a Wails marshalling quirk or a future Go change returning nil,nil) is therefore indistinguishable from a genuinely empty machine list — the same load-failure-vs-empty confusion the team already fixed elsewhere in commit 7c6737f.
fix: Treat a null resolution as a load failure: in load(), if (seq===loadSeq) { if (d) data = d; else error = 'Could not load machines'; }. Or tighten the bridge type to Promise<MachinesDTO> since the Go method always returns a non-nil DTO on success, and let the catch handle real errors.

title: Detected-only hosts show a misleading install hint and no signal that they may not run eigen at all
problem: ssh_config detection surfaces EVERY Host alias as a machine card, including hosts that have nothing to do with eigen (a git server, a bastion, a NAS). Clicking such a card dials ssh and runs `eigen daemon stdio`; ListSessions returns the dial.go fallback 'no eigen on <host> — install it'. The sheet then shows 'Install with eigen remote install' — actively encouraging the user to push an eigen daemon onto an arbitrary box that happened to be in their ssh config. There is no distinction in the card between a saved eigen remote (known to run eigen) and a bare detected alias (eigen presence unknown), beyond a tiny 'detected' badge, and the error copy assumes the user WANTS eigen there.
fix: Soften the detected-only path: in the remote-error hint, when the machine is detected-only (!m.saved), phrase it as 'This ssh host may not run eigen' rather than commanding an install, and/or gate the install hint behind m.saved. Consider visually separating saved eigen remotes from raw ssh_config aliases (a section or a clearer label) so users don't treat every config Host as an eigen target.

title: base() dir helper collapses distinct remote dirs and mishandles root/trailing-slash paths
problem: base(dir) strips a trailing slash, splits on '/', and returns the last segment. For '/' it yields '' then falls back to dir ('/'); for '/srv/' it returns 'srv' — fine. But two remote sessions in /home/a/work and /opt/b/work both render as 'work' with no disambiguation, and the full path is only available via the title= tooltip (invisible to keyboard/touch and screen readers). Windows-style or URL-ish remote dirs (backslashes, or a path with no '/') return the whole string unsplit. It also doesn't guard a dir that is purely slashes ('//') which yields '' twice and falls through to dir. The remote list is the one place users compare sessions across a machine, so identical basenames are a real readability hit.
fix: Keep the basename for the primary label but make the full dir reachable without a tooltip — render it in a secondary muted line (as the card's .mc__mono dir does) or make the row expandable. At minimum normalize backslashes and handle empty/root by showing the original dir. Reuse a shared path-basename util rather than a per-view regex so behavior matches the card's dir rendering.

title: RemoteSessions opens a fresh ssh process per drill with no connection reuse or in-flight coalescing
file: internal/remote/dial.go:77-97 (ListSessions: Dial then immediate Close) + Machines.svelte:56-70 (drill)
problem: Every drill-in spins up a brand-new `ssh host eigen daemon stdio` subprocess, lists once, and tears it down. Re-opening the same machine's sheet (or closing and reopening to retry) pays the full ssh handshake + remote daemon spawn cost again — which on a cold remote can be seconds (the daemon 'new'/cold-start path is minutes-capable). There is also no guard against the user clicking a card while a prior dial to that same machine is still resolving: drill() bumps remoteSeq and discards the stale result, but the underlying ssh subprocess from the abandoned dial keeps running to completion (it isn't cancelled — see the no-context issue). Rapid card clicks fan out N concurrent ssh processes.
fix: At the Go seam, optionally cache a short-lived ssh connection per target for re-list within a few seconds, or at least coalesce concurrent ListSessions(target) calls. At the view, debounce/guard drill() so re-dialing the already-open machine while remoteLoading is a no-op. Pairing this with the ConnectTimeout + cancellation fix above bounds the worst case.

title: Dialing spinner conveys progress only visually — no aria-live / aria-busy for screen readers
problem: The 'Dialing over ssh…' state is a span with the spinner marked aria-hidden and the text in a plain span. There's no aria-live region or role=status, so a screen-reader user opening the sheet hears the title/ssh but gets no announcement that a slow operation is in progress, nor when it completes/fails. Given the dial can take up to ~30s, the silence is a real gap. The subsequent remote-error block and the empty/list states likewise aren't announced on transition.
fix: Wrap the dialing/result region in role="status" aria-live="polite" (and aria-busy={remoteLoading} on the body) so the 'Dialing over ssh…' text and the resolved outcome are announced. The reduced-motion fallback for the spinner already exists; only the live-region semantics are missing.

title: Card title tooltip says 'Dial … for live sessions' even when the host likely has no eigen daemon
problem: Every card's hover title promises 'Dial <ssh> for live sessions', but for a detected-only ssh_config alias with no eigen installed, dialing yields an error, not sessions. The tooltip overpromises for the exact case (bare detected hosts) that is most likely to fail, compounding the install-hint UX issue.
fix: Make the title conditional: for saved hosts keep 'Dial … for live sessions'; for detected-only hosts use 'Check <ssh> for an eigen daemon' or similar so the affordance's promise matches the likely outcome.

title: No daemon-offline guard on the Machines view despite the rest of the GUI gating on daemon status
problem: Machines() and RemoteSessions() run server-side in the GUI process and don't require the LOCAL daemon to be online (RemoteSessions spawns its own ssh→remote daemon). That's actually correct — but the view gives the user zero context about it. Sibling views surface daemon online/offline; here, if the local daemon is down the user sees a fully-functional Machines board, which is fine, yet there's also no banner clarifying that remote dialing is independent of local daemon health. Conversely, nothing communicates that a remote dial reaches OUT over ssh rather than through the local daemon. Minor, but the mental model is unclear at a glance.
fix: Low effort: a one-line muted note in the header (or in the sheet) clarifying that dialing reaches the remote host directly over ssh and works regardless of the local daemon. No functional change needed; this is purely orientation copy to prevent users from assuming the board is broken when their local daemon is offline.

title: Turn error/interrupt never clears the GUI's running state — chat wedges on "working…" forever
problem: When a turn ends abnormally the daemon's finishTurn() emits ONLY an EventNote ("error: <msg>" on failure, "interrupted" on cancel) and NOT an EventDone — see internal/daemon/session.go:348-353. The frontend transcript store clears `running` ONLY in the `done` case; the `note` case pushes a NoteBlock and leaves `running` untouched. So after any provider error, context-overflow-that-couldn't-compact, reasoning-only spin-out (agent.go:1324/1370), or user Interrupt, store.running stays true indefinitely. Chat.svelte's reconciliation effect ($effect at line 105-107) only calls refreshState() when `store.running === false`, so it never fires — the "working…" indicator (Chat.svelte:399), the Composer's steer-mode (line 408), and the disabled Compact button (line 433) all stay stuck until the user navigates away and back (which re-seeds from State). The Interrupt button is especially broken: clicking it cancels the daemon turn, the daemon emits "interrupted" as a note, but the UI keeps showing working forever.
fix: In transcript.svelte.ts onEvent, treat a terminal note as turn-ending: in the `note` case, after pushing the NoteBlock, set `running = false` (the daemon only emits a standalone note as the terminal event for error/interrupt; the in-turn informational notes like compaction always have a following `done`). Safer: have the daemon emit a real terminal signal — add an EventError kind (or set a terminal flag on the note) in finishTurn so the frontend can distinguish a terminal note from an in-turn note and clear running deterministically. Minimal frontend-only fix: in the `note` case, if `running`, schedule a refreshState by also clearing running so the existing running===false effect fires and re-seeds authoritative state.

title: Non-streaming providers' final answer is dropped from the live stream (EventDone.text discarded)
problem: For a provider that does NOT implement llm.Streamer (the comment at agent.go:1286 names the Converse/opus path), the final-answer branch emits ONLY EventDone{Text: resp.Text} with no preceding EventTextDelta — the only EventTextDelta-on-final emit (agent.go:1349) is gated behind `!streamed && s.hasSteer()`. The frontend `done` case flushes pending + commits live + clears running but never appends e.text, so the assistant's final prose is not rendered incrementally. It is recovered only because clearing running triggers Chat.svelte's refreshState()→State() full reseed; the live block shows nothing until that round-trip resolves, causing a visible empty gap and an O(history) reseed on every non-streaming turn instead of a cheap append.
fix: In the transcript store `done` case, when e.text is non-empty and no live/pending text for this step has been shown (i.e. the provider didn't stream), push a final text block: `if (e.text && !streamedThisTurn) pushHistory({uid: nextUid(), kind:'text', step: e.step??0, text: e.text})` before clearing running. Track a `sawDelta` flag reset per turn (set in the text/reasoning case, cleared on done) so streamed turns don't double-render. Alternatively have the daemon always emit the final text as a delta for non-streamers.

title: Error notes render identically to benign notes — failures look like ordinary status lines
problem:
finishTurn emits failures and interrupts as plain EventNote with Text "error: " / "interrupted", which the frontend renders as `
{block.text}
` — the same neutral styling used for benign informational notes ("task → background", "context auto-compacted…", goal-achieved). A provider failure (rate limit, auth error, context overflow that couldn't compact) is visually indistinguishable from a routine note, and the only signal is the literal "error: " prefix in the text. There is no error tone, icon, or retry affordance, and (combined with the stuck-running bug) the user may not realize the turn failed at all.
fix: Carry the error/severity through the event contract instead of relying on a string prefix: add an EventError kind (or reuse IsError on the note WireEvent) in finishTurn for the err!=nil branch, mirror it in WireEventDTO, and add a NoteBlock `tone` ('error'|'info') in the transcript store. Render error notes with the error token styling (and ideally a Resend affordance, since Bridge.Resend already exists). Minimal stopgap: in Chat.svelte detect a leading "error:" in note text and apply an error class.

title: WireEventDTO drops provider/model/cache-token fields the agent sets on EventDone — GUI can't show per-turn provider/model or cache hit-rate live
problem: agent.Event carries Provider, Model, CacheReadTokens, CacheWriteTokens on EventDone (internal/agent/agent.go:90-101, populated at agent.go:1358). The daemon's WireEvent and thus WireEventDTO/types.ts WireEventDTO only forward InTokens/OutTokens — Provider, Model, and cache tokens are silently dropped on the live stream. So the GUI's live transcript can never display which provider/model produced a turn nor the prompt-cache hit rate (CacheRead vs In), the headline token-efficiency signal the codebase explicitly tracks. The data is only reachable via the aggregate daemon stats stream, not per-turn. (Root cause is daemon-side; ledger APP-066 covers adding the fields to daemon WireEvent. This is the downstream GUI DTO/types.ts mirror that must follow.)
fix: After APP-066 adds Provider/Model/CacheReadTokens/CacheWriteTokens to daemon.WireEvent, add the same omitempty fields to WireEventDTO in dto.go (mapped in toWireEventDTO), mirror them in types.ts WireEventDTO, and surface provider/model + cache-hit on the done block (e.g. a meta footer on the completed assistant turn). Purely additive.

title: fromMessageDTOs round-trip is dead in production (only exercised by tests) yet asymmetrically drops reasoning identity fields
problem: fromMessageDTOs (and the MessageDTO->llm.Message direction) is never called by any Bridge method — the GUI only reads State() (Go->TS) and sends fresh input as text; it never ships MessageDTO[] back to Go. Its only caller is bridge_test.go. The helper also can't round-trip faithfully: llm.Message has ReasoningID and ReasoningEncrypted (llm.go:51-57, load-bearing for Codex/Responses store:false continuity) which MessageDTO omits entirely, so any future use of fromMessageDTOs would silently lose encrypted-reasoning continuity. The toMessageDTO direction is display-only so the omission is harmless there, but the dead reverse helper invites a future correctness bug.
fix: Delete fromMessageDTOs (and fromImageDTOs' message-level use) and its test if no Bridge method needs the TS->Go message direction; the seam is read-only for history. If a future feature does need it (e.g. importing a transcript), first add ReasoningID/ReasoningEncrypted to MessageDTO + types.ts so the round-trip is lossless.

title: daemon DaemonStats is emitted raw (un-DTO'd) on the stats event, so types.ts must mirror Go json tags exactly with no compile-time guard
problem: Unlike every other surface, the daemon stats path bypasses the DTO layer: bridge.go emits the raw *daemon.DaemonStats (snake_case Go json tags) both on the eventDaemonStats stream (line 106) and from Stats() (line 228). types.ts hand-mirrors those snake_case tags. There is no DTO struct decoupling the wire shape, so a daemon-side field rename/add (the identity fields executable/binary_sha256/vcs_revision/vcs_modified added at protocol.go:82-85) drifts silently from types.ts with zero TS or Go signal — types.ts DaemonStats is already missing all four identity fields (ledger GUI-045). The mixing of snake_case (DaemonStats) and camelCase (every DTO) at the same seam is also an inconsistency that makes the parity rule ("keep in sync with dto.go") not actually cover this type. (Related to GUI-045, which covers the specific missing identity fields; this is the architectural no-DTO drift root cause.)
fix: Either introduce a DaemonStatsDTO with the project's camelCase convention and a toDaemonStatsDTO mapper (consistent with the rest of dto.go, and a single place to keep parity), or at minimum document in types.ts that DaemonStats mirrors daemon.DaemonStats's snake_case tags directly and add the four missing identity fields. The DTO approach is the consistent fix.

title: Plugin scan findings (what was flagged on a forced install) are dropped at the DTO — GUI shows 'scan: forced' but never why
problem: InstalledPlugin carries Scans []ScanFinding (per-component {Component, Reasons}), explicitly kept 'for audit/UI' (registry.go:51). The TUI app renders these (internal/app/plugins.go:992-994 lists each flagged component + its reasons). The GUI's InstalledPluginDTO omits Scans entirely, so a plugin installed with --force shows only a yellow 'scan: forced' badge. The user cannot see WHICH component (skill:x / command:y / agent:z) tripped the scanner or the reasons — exactly the security-relevant audit trail that justifies surfacing scan status at all. ScanCount is also carried in the DTO but never rendered (GUI-054 notes scanCount; this is the deeper Scans gap, not in the ledger). (Related to GUI-054, which notes the unrendered scanCount; this is the deeper Scans[] audit-trail gap.)
fix: Add `Scans []ScanFindingDTO` (Component string; Reasons []string) to InstalledPluginDTO and populate it from p.Scans in Plugins(). In Plugins.svelte, when scanStatus === 'forced', render an expandable detail under the row listing each Component and its Reasons (mirror internal/app/plugins.go:992). Add the type to types.ts.

title: Config select fields (model / judge_model) can never be cleared back to empty/'automatic' from the GUI
problem: model and judge_model render as <select> populated from the dynamic model catalog (dynamicOptions('models')), which contains no empty option. judge_model treats empty as a valid, meaningful value ('automatic cross-vendor judge' per fields.go:32). The select only injects a synthetic <option> for the CURRENT value when it isn't in the list (lines 155-157): once the user picks a real model, the empty value disappears from the option set and there is no clear/reset affordance and no free-text fallback (it's a closed select). So a user who sets judge_model can never return it to automatic via the GUI — the capability is one-way.
fix: For dynamic-option fields that accept empty (judge_model, and arguably model), prepend an explicit option like `<option value="">(automatic)</option>` to the select, or render these as a combobox/free-text-with-suggestions so an empty/unset value is reachable. Mark such fields as clearable in ConfigFieldDTO (e.g. a `clearable`/`allowEmpty` flag) so the view knows to offer the empty option.

title: Crons collapses four distinct timer states into one 'active = next>0' flag, so Stop/Disable give no visible result
problem: CronDTO.Active is set to `t.Next > 0` (whether systemd reports a future run). The view maps this single boolean to FOUR verbs: active → [Stop, Disable], inactive → [Start, Enable]. But enabled-vs-running are independent in systemd. 'Stop' (`systemctl --user stop`) halts only the current activation; an *enabled* timer still reports a future Next, so after Stop the row reloads, still shows next>0, still labels 'scheduled' and still shows Stop/Disable — the user sees no change and may think the action failed (the toast says 'stop <name>' regardless). Conversely 'Enable' on a unit that has no calendar trigger won't produce a Next, so it flips right back to Start/Enable. The control set doesn't reflect the actual enabled/active state, so two of the four actions routinely appear to do nothing.
fix: Surface real state: have loadSystemdTimers also read each unit's enabled state (`systemctl --user is-enabled <unit>`) and active/running state, add `Enabled bool` (and keep Active for running) to CronDTO. In Crons.svelte show Enable/Disable driven by Enabled and Start/Stop driven by the running state independently (two separate controls), so each verb maps to the state it actually changes. At minimum, after ctl() re-derive which buttons to show from the refreshed enabled+active pair rather than next>0 alone.

title: Plugins view never refreshes after out-of-band installs/uninstalls and leaves stale inline-confirm state across reloads
problem: Plugins.svelte loads once on mount with no live event subscription and no refresh-on-focus/route-re-entry (the same class of gap APP-039 fixed for Skills, but Plugins was never covered). A plugin installed via the CLI (`eigen plugin install`) — the only documented install path, per the view's own header comment — will not appear until the app is restarted or the view is force-remounted. Separately, `confirming` (the inline Uninstall?/Remove? key) is module-level $state that load() never resets: if a background reload fires while a row is mid-confirm, the confirm UI can point at a row whose data changed, or persist against a row that no longer exists.
fix: Subscribe to a plugins-changed signal (emit an `eigen:plugins` event after RemovePlugin/SetMarketEnabled/RemoveMarketplace, and ideally after a CLI install if the daemon can signal) and call load() on it; at minimum re-load on window focus / route re-entry. Reset `confirming = null` at the top of load() so a refresh clears any dangling inline confirm.

title: config.Set never validates the 'effort' closed-set the GUI advertises, so the select silently trusts an unenforced contract
problem: Fields() declares effort as a CLOSED option set (off|minimal|none|low|medium|high|xhigh|max) and the GUI renders it as a <select> on that basis. But config.Set's effort case is `c.Effort = value` with no validation — every other closed field (perm, input_mode, theme, nerd_font, the bool fields) validates and returns an error on a bad value.
