The GUI happens to constrain effort through the select, so the missing backend guard is invisible today; but the Fields()-declares-closed / Set()-accepts-anything mismatch means the GUI is relying on an unenforced contract (and any other caller, or a future free-text path, can write garbage that the picker then has to special-case via the synthetic-option branch at Config.svelte:155).
fix: Add validation to the effort case in config.Set mirroring the other closed fields: reject values not in the Fields() option set (allow empty for 'unset'), returning a clear error. This makes the closed-set contract real and keeps the GUI select and backend in agreement.

title: Config toggle and multi-select chips give no in-flight feedback and chips show no optimistic state
problem: GUI-052 (ledger) covers the missing saving indicator for <select>, but the boolean toggle and the route_providers chips have the same gap and are not covered: while saving[f.key] is true they are merely `disabled` with no spinner/dim, so a slow daemon commit looks frozen. Additionally toggleMulti() commits the new set but does not optimistically update values[f.key]; multiHas() keeps reading the OLD value until the round-trip resolves, so a toggled chip does not visually change (and is disabled) for the whole commit — the click appears to do nothing on a slow link. (Related to GUI-052, which covers the select saving indicator; this extends it to the toggle and chips.)
fix: Show the same lightweight 'saving…'/dimmed affordance for the toggle and chips when saving[f.key]. For chips, set values[f.key] to the optimistic joined set before awaiting commit (commit already reverts on error), so the chip reflects the pending toggle immediately.

title: Skills proposal review strip is a horizontal scroll region unreachable by keyboard
problem: .strip__row is `overflow-x: auto` with scroll-snap and fixed-width 268px proposal cards laid out left-to-right. It has no tabindex and is not a focusable scroll container, so a keyboard/AT user cannot scroll the queue horizontally; proposals that overflow the viewport width are only reachable if they happen to fall before the cut, and the '+N more' button only appends below the page size — it does not bring already-rendered-but-off-screen cards into view. The Accept/Reject buttons on off-screen cards are tab-focusable but won't auto-scroll into view on focus in all engines.
fix: Make the scroll region keyboard-operable: add `tabindex="0"` + a role/label to .strip__row (or handle ArrowLeft/Right to scroll), and on focus of a proposal's button call scrollIntoView({block:'nearest', inline:'nearest'}) so tabbing through Accept/Reject reveals each card. Alternatively wrap to a vertical/grid layout under a width breakpoint.

title: Plugin Uninstall and Marketplace Remove fire on a single inline confirm with no consequence preview, despite being fully destructive
problem: RemovePlugin reverses ALL of a plugin's wiring (skills, agents, mcp, hooks, commands) and deletes its files (plugins.go comment + registry.Uninstall → uninstallFiles). The GUI guards it with a one-click inline 'Uninstall? Confirm' but shows no summary of what will be removed — even though the row already has the component counts (3 skills · 2 agents · …) and the DTO carries the exact names. A user confirming has no reminder that, say, 3 skills they rely on will vanish. Marketplace Remove is lighter (registry record only) but uses the identical confirm affordance, so the two destructive weights read as equivalent.
fix: In the confirm state for a plugin, replace the bare 'Uninstall?' with a one-line consequence summary built from components(p) (e.g. 'Remove 3 skills, 2 agents, 1 mcp?'). Keep the marketplace confirm lighter-weight so the visual weight matches the actual blast radius.

title: StatusDot is always aria-hidden, so color-only session/agent/tool status is invisible to screen readers
problem: The dot is hard-coded `aria-hidden="true"` (line 25) at every call site. In several views it is the ONLY status carrier: Sessions rows (working/idle/error/approval conveyed only by dot color + a non-text left-border tint), Routing provider rail (credentialed vs not = green/grey dot only), Plugins marketplace rows when enabled (the 'disabled' Badge only renders when disabled, so 'enabled' = green dot alone), Crons timer active state, Machines remote rows, ToolCallCard header. A non-sighted or color-blind user gets no equivalent. This is the systematic color-only-status gap across the whole app.
fix: Give StatusDot an optional accessible label: accept a `label?: string` prop (or derive a default per state — 'working'/'idle'/'error'/'needs approval'/'ok'), and when provided render the span with `role="img"` + `aria-label={label}` instead of `aria-hidden`. Pass a label from the SOLE-indicator sites (Sessions/Routing/Plugins/Crons/Machines rows). Keep aria-hidden only where adjacent visible text already names the state (e.g. TopBar/Rail footer have a word beside the dot).

title: Chat transcript has no live-region semantics — streamed assistant text and tool results are never announced
problem: The transcript scroll region (`.chat__scroll` → VirtualList at lines 350-396) carries no `role="log"`/`aria-live`. New assistant tokens, reasoning, notes, and tool-call cards append silently; a screen-reader user gets zero notification that the agent replied or that a turn finished. Compounding it, message rows have no role distinguishing user vs assistant vs tool vs reasoning (all are plain `<div class="msg ...">`), so even on manual review the conversation reads as an undifferentiated text run. The only spoken signal is the 'working…' label, which itself lacks a live region. (Merged from a duplicate finding: the .msg--live block and the 'working…' indicator are plain divs with no aria-live; turn start/end is never announced to AT.)
fix: Add `role="log" aria-label="Conversation" aria-live="polite" aria-relevant="additions text"` to the transcript container (or a wrapper around VirtualList). Note VirtualList only mounts visible rows, so for reliable announcement add an offscreen `aria-live` mirror of the latest finalized assistant block. Give each `.chat__row` a role/label (e.g. `role="article"` with an aria-label naming the speaker: 'eigen', 'you', 'tool: <name>', 'reasoning'). Also give the 'working…' status a visually-hidden aria-live announcement ('agent is working' / 'reply ready') so turn start/end is announced (use aria-live=polite to avoid interrupting on every token).

title: Markdown task-list checkboxes are aria-hidden with no text alternative — checked/unchecked is color-only
problem: Rendered GFM task lists draw the checkbox as a decorative `<span class="md-task" aria-hidden="true">` (lines 159-165) whose done/not-done state is conveyed purely by a brand fill + a CSS ::after checkmark. A screen reader hears the item text with no indication of whether the task is complete — the single most meaningful bit of a checklist is dropped. This appears wherever assistant prose or memory notes contain a `- [ ] / - [x]` list.
fix: Replace the decorative span with semantic state: render a real `<input type="checkbox" checked={item.checked} disabled>` (native checkbox is announced as checked/unchecked), or keep the styled span but add `role="checkbox" aria-checked={!!item.checked} aria-label={item.checked ? 'done' : 'to do'}` and drop aria-hidden. Keep it non-interactive (disabled / no handler) since these are read-only.

title: Config boolean toggle and route_providers chip controls have no reduced-motion guard
problem: The `.toggle__knob` slide (`transform/background` transition, lines 285-288) and the `.chip` color/border/background transitions (lines 364-368) animate on every value change, but the view's `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 381-385) only stills `.cfg__skel` — it does not neutralize the toggle knob or chip transitions. A user who asked for reduced motion still gets the sliding knob. (Other views are careful here; Config is the gap.)
fix: Extend the reduced-motion block to add `.toggle__knob, .toggle, .chip { transition: none; }` (and `.toggle--on .toggle__knob { transform: translateX(18px); }` is fine as a static end-state). Mirrors how Live/Home/Card hold their end-states statically under reduced motion.

title: Hand-rolled modal scrims (role=button) only handle Enter, not Space — and several aren't reachable as the spec implies
problem: Every dismiss-scrim built as `<div role="button" tabindex="-1" onkeydown={(e)=>e.key==="Enter" && close()}>` handles only Enter. ARIA `role="button"` is expected to activate on BOTH Enter and Space; a keyboard user pressing Space on a focused scrim gets nothing. Same pattern is copy-pasted in CommandPalette.svelte (line 190), Popover.svelte (line 74), Shortcuts.svelte (line 47), and the per-view sheets in Skills/Agents/Dreaming. The scrims are also `tabindex="-1"` (not keyboard-focusable), so the only real keyboard dismiss is Escape — meaning the `role="button"` + keydown is misleading dead semantics.
fix: Either (a) drop the role=button/keydown from the scrims entirely (they're decorative click-catchers; Escape already closes via the dialog's handler and trapFocus keeps focus inside), leaving just `onclick`; or (b) if kept as buttons, handle Space too: `(e)=>(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" ") && close()`. Option (a) is cleaner and matches that the scrim is never tab-focusable.

title: Home feed-card dismiss (×) icon button has an aria-label but no visible focus indicator
problem: The `.fc__x` dismiss button (used on both Act-on cards, line 154, and Ideas cards, line 182) is an icon-only `×`. It correctly carries `aria-label="Dismiss"`, but the stylesheet defines only `.fc__x:hover` (lines 415-418) — there is no `.fc__x:focus-visible` rule. A keyboard user tabbing through the feed cannot see which dismiss control is focused (no ring, no background), unlike every other interactive control in the app which gets `box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus)`.
fix: Add `.fc__x:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); color: var(--text-primary); }` mirroring the other focus-visible treatments in the codebase.

title: Skills proposal hover/scroll transitions lack a reduced-motion guard
problem: The view's reduced-motion block (lines 700-709) stills the sheet/scrim animations and `.skills__skel` + `.strip__pulse`, but leaves two motion sources running: `.sk__rail` `transition: opacity` (lines 566-570, which animates on card hover) and `.prop--more` `transition: background` (line 488). Minor compared to the breathing pulses, but it's an inconsistency with the otherwise-thorough reduced-motion handling in this same file.
fix: Add `.sk__rail, .prop--more { transition: none; }` to the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block.

title: Memory and Routing segmented/provider controls have unguarded color transitions under reduced motion
problem: Memory's `.mem__scope` segmented tabs animate `background`/`color` (lines 293-296) and have NO `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule for them — the file's only reduced-motion entry stills `.mem__skel` (lines 486-490). Routing's `.prov` rail buttons (lines 247-250) and `.route__search` border transition similarly fall outside its reduced-motion block (which only stills `.route__skel` and `.hbar__track span`). These are short color fades so the impact is small, but a strict reduced-motion preference should suppress them for consistency with Live/Home/Observe which do.
fix: Add the segmented/rail/search transitions to each view's reduced-motion block, e.g. Memory `.mem__scope { transition: none; }` and Routing `.prov, .route__search { transition: none; }`.

title: Composer clears draft+images before send RPC resolves — data loss on failure
Custom slash commands (Commands/RunCommand) built but unreachable from GUI
Authored workflows (Workflows/RunWorkflow) built but no GUI bridge facade
Wails per-event goroutine dispatch can reorder the session stream; transcript trusts arrival order
Clear conversation leaves old transcript on screen (no re-seed)
Synchronous daemon spawn in ServiceStartup blocks the window painting on cold start
